chapters.name: The Living World
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Taxon is a:
questions.question_raw: Taxon is a:
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Ranking.

questions.question: The expansion of ICBN is:
questions.question_raw: The expansion of ICBN is:
questions.explanation: The scientific names are based on agreed principles and criteria, which are provided in the International Code for Botanical Nomenclature (ICBN). Hence, the correct option is the International code of botanical nomenclature.

questions.question: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.question_raw: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.explanation: Animal taxonomists have evolved the International Code of Zoological Nomenclature (ICZN). Hence, the correct option is the International code of zoological nomenclature.

questions.question: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.question_raw: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.explanation: The first word in a biological name represents the genus while the second component denotes the specific epithet. Hence, the correct option is Two.

questions.question: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:
questions.question_raw: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:

options.label: A
options.text: The name of the author who first described the formation

options.label: B
options.text: The name of the author who first described the species.

options.label: C
options.text: The name of the author who first described the genus.

options.label: D
options.text: The name of the author who first described the subspecies.

correct_option_texts: The name of the author who first described the species.
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica Linn, the word ‘Linn’ indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is the name of the author who first described the species.

questions.question: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.question_raw: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.explanation: Mangifera Indica. Here, Mangifera is a generic name while Indica is a specific name. Hence, the correct option is the Genus species.

questions.question: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.question_raw: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.explanation: Since biology deals with both plants and animals, the international code of biological nomenclature is applied to both plants and animals. Hence, the correct option is Both plant and animals.

questions.question: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.question_raw: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.explanation: The basics of taxonomy are the identification, naming, and classification of organisms are universally evolved under international codes. Hence, the correct option is Identification.

questions.question: Which one is the exclusive characteristic of a living organism?
questions.question_raw: Which one is the exclusive characteristic of a living organism?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolated metabolic reaction occuring in vitro.

options.label: B
options.text: Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory

options.label: C
options.text: Increase in mass both from outside and inside

correct_option_texts: Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory
questions.explanation: The most obvious and technically complicated feature of all living organisms is this ability to sense their surroundings or environment and respond to these environmental stimuli which could be physical, chemical or biological. We sense our environment through our sense organs. Plants respond to external factors like light, water, temperature, other organisms, pollutants, etc. All organisms, from the prokaryotes to the most complex eukaryotes can sense and respond to environmental cues. Hence, the correct option is the Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory.

questions.question: Living things differ from non-living things in a manner that:
questions.question_raw: Living things differ from non-living things in a manner that:

options.label: A
options.text: Living things respond to the physical level

options.label: B
options.text: Living things have a different level of organisation

options.label: C
options.text: Living things are more in member than non-living

correct_option_texts: Living things have a different level of organisation
questions.explanation: The cellular level organisation of the body is the defining feature of life forms or living things. Hence, the correct option is Living things have a different level of organisation.

questions.question:
Sometimes different authors give different names to one and the same species. In such a situation:
 The name under which the species was first described is a valid name.
 The latest name under which the species was described is valid.
 People can use any one of the names for the species.
 All known names are discarded and a new name is given to the species.
questions.question_raw:
Sometimes different authors give different names to one and the same species. In such a situation:

  1. The name under which the species was first described is a valid name.
  2. The latest name under which the species was described is valid.
  3. People can use any one of the names for the species.
  4. All known names are discarded and a new name is given to the species.
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica Linn, the word ‘Linn’ indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is The name under which the species was first described is the valid name.

questions.question: Hierarchical classification refers:
questions.question_raw: Hierarchical classification refers:

options.label: D
options.text: To rank one above the other

correct_option_texts: To rank one above the other
questions.explanation: Classification is not a single step process but involves a hierarchy of steps in which each step represents a rank or category. Hence, the correct option is To rank one above the other.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?
 The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
 Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
 Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?

  1. The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
  2. Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
  3. Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.explanation: All of these are the universal rules of binomial nomenclature. Hence, the correct option is All of these.

questions.question: In a scientific name, the name of the author is printed in ________.
questions.question_raw: In a scientific name, the name of the author is printed in ________.
questions.explanation: The name of the author appears after the specific epithet, i.e., at the end of the biological name and is written in an abbreviated form, e.g., Mangifera indica Linn. It indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is Abbreviated.

questions.question: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.question_raw: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.explanation: The characterization, identification, classification, and nomenclature are the processes that are basic to taxonomy as well as systematics. Hence, the correct option is Taxonomy and systematics.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question:
Which is not the universal rule of nomenclature?
 Biological names are generally in Greek.
 The biological names are printed in italics.
 Genus name starts with a capital letter while the species name starts with small letters.
 The first word represents the genus and the second word represents species.
questions.question_raw:
Which is not the universal rule of nomenclature?

  1. Biological names are generally in Greek.
  2. The biological names are printed in italics.
  3. Genus name starts with a capital letter while the species name starts with small letters.
  4. The first word represents the genus and the second word represents species.
questions.explanation: According to the universal rule of nomenclature, the biological names are generally in Latin and written in italics. Hence, the correct option is Biological names are generally in Greek.

questions.question: The number of species that are known and described is:
questions.question_raw: The number of species that are known and described is:
questions.explanation: Each different kind of plant, animal, or organism represents a species. The number of species known and described ranges between 1.7-1.8 million. This refers to biodiversity or the number and types of organisms present on earth. Hence, the correct option is 1.7 - 1.8 million.

questions.question: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.question_raw: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.explanation: Born on 5 July 1904, in Kempten, Germany, ERNST MAYR, the Harvard University evolutionary biologist who has been called ‘The Darwin of the 20th century’, was one of the 100 greatest scientists of all time.

questions.question: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:
questions.question_raw: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:

options.label: D
options.text: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome

correct_option_texts: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome
questions.explanation: Virus differs from bacteria in terms of having DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosomes. The virus can replicate inside the host cell but become inactive outside the host cell.

questions.question: Wildlife is destroyed most when:
questions.question_raw: Wildlife is destroyed most when:

options.label: A
options.text: There is lack of proper care

questions.explanation: Wildlife is destroyed most when natural habitat is destroyed. The organisms survive and reproduce only in the natural habitat. The loss of habitat of wild animals is the main reason behind the extinction of organisms.

questions.question: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.question_raw: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.explanation: The animals that were present earlier but now not available are called extinct animals. In India, a number of animals have become extinct such as Cheetah.

questions.question: In which organism protoplasm and cellular structure is absent ______.
questions.question_raw: In which organism protoplasm and cellular structure is absent ______.
questions.explanation: The protoplasm and cellular structure are absent in Virus. A virus consists of genetic material (DNA or RNA) along with a capsule of protein without any cellular structure.

questions.question: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.question_raw: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.explanation: The method in which living organism show internal growth due to the addition of materials fand formation of cells inside the body is called intussusception.

questions.question: Example of interspecific hybrid is:
questions.question_raw: Example of interspecific hybrid is:
questions.explanation: Tigon and liger are produced by hybridization of tow interspecific species. Although hybridization generally takes place between the same species.

questions.question: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.question_raw: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.explanation: All organisms, from primitive prokaryotes to most advanced and complex eukaryotes, are able to sense and respond to environmental factors. The stimuli are perceived by sense organs in higher animals through sensory receptors e.g., eyes, ears, nose. Plants do not possess such sense organs. However, they do respond to external factors such as light, water, temperature, pollutants, other organisms, etc. Human beings have an additional facility of self-consciousness (awareness of self). Consciousness and response to stimuli are said to be the defining properties of living organisms.

questions.question: The number of categories in the hierarchical classification is ______.
questions.question_raw: The number of categories in the hierarchical classification is ______.
questions.explanation: Taxonomical studies of all known organisms have led to the development of common categories such as kingdom, phylum (for animals) or division (for plants), class, order, family, genus, and species. Hence, the correct option is 7.

questions.question: In a taxonomic hierarchy, the various categories are arranged in:
questions.question_raw: In a taxonomic hierarchy, the various categories are arranged in:
questions.explanation:
Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species.
Hence, the correct option is Descending order.

questions.question: Choose the correct sequence of taxonomic categories in descending order:
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct sequence of taxonomic categories in descending order:

options.label: A
options.text: Division - Order - Family - Class - Genus - Species

options.label: B
options.text: Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species

options.label: C
options.text: Division - Family - Class - Order - Genus - Species

options.label: D
options.text: Division - Class - Order - Genus - Family - Species

correct_option_texts: Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species
questions.explanation: The correct sequence of taxonomic categories form higher to lower are Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species.

questions.question: A group of closely related families is called ______.
questions.question_raw: A group of closely related families is called ______.
questions.explanation: Class includes a group of related orders, Genus comprises a group of related species, species is a basic unit of classification. Hence, the correct option is Order.

questions.question: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.question_raw: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.explanation:
The taxonomic hierarchy Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species.
Hence, the correct option is the Kingdom.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.explanation: Genus comprises a group of related species, Family has a group of related genera, the order has a group of related family. Hence, the correct option is Phylum.

questions.question: The basic unit of classification is ______.
questions.question_raw: The basic unit of classification is ______.
questions.explanation: The basic unit of classification is species. Species are the population of organisms that can interbreed among themselves to produce new organisms.

questions.question: Related genera belong to the same ________.
questions.question_raw: Related genera belong to the same ________.
questions.explanation: A family is a group of genera having some common properties. So, related genera belong to the same family.

questions.question: A fundamental category of classification ranking below a genus is_______.
questions.question_raw: A fundamental category of classification ranking below a genus is_______.
questions.explanation: The taxonomic hierarchy Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species. Hence, the correct option is Species.

questions.question: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.question_raw: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.explanation: Plant families like Convolvulaceae, Solanaceae are included in the order Polymoniales mainly based on the floral characters. Hence, the correct option is Family.

questions.question: Triticum aestivum belong to the family _______.
questions.question_raw: Triticum aestivum belong to the family _______.
questions.explanation: Triticum aestivum, common or bread wheat, is an annual grass in the Poaceae (grass family) native to the Mediterranean region and southwest Asia.

questions.question: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Taxon.

questions.question: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.explanation: The biological name of an organism consists of the genus name and species name. the correct biological nomenclature of Mango is Mangifer indica Linn.

questions.question: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?
questions.question_raw: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?

options.label: A
options.text: The phylum is a more inclusive category

options.label: B
options.text: Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals

options.label: C
options.text: Division is a more inclusive category

options.label: D
options.text: Phylum is more exclusive than division

correct_option_texts: Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals
questions.explanation: In the case of plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called division, whereas phylum was used to refer to animals. Hence, the correct option is Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals.

questions.question: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.question_raw: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.explanation: Houseflies are insects of the order Diptera, the name is derived from the Greek di = two, and ptera = wings

questions.question:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?
 Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
 A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
 Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
 Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.question_raw:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?

  1. Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
  2. A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
  3. Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
  4. Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.explanation: Lion (Panthera leo), leopard (P. pardus), and tiger (P. Tigris) with several common features are all species of the genus Panthera. The genus Felisincludes cats. Hence, the correct option is Lion, tiger, leopard and cat belong to the same genus.

questions.question: What is the scientific name of wheat?
questions.question_raw: What is the scientific name of wheat?
questions.explanation: Wheat is a grass widely cultivated for its seed, a cereal grain which is a worldwide staple food. There are many species of wheat which together make up the genus Triticum; the most widely grown is common wheat (T. aestivum).

questions.question: A true species consist of a population:
questions.question_raw: A true species consist of a population:
questions.explanation:
As per the definition given by Ernst Mayr in 1964: "A species is a group of actually or potentially inbreeding populations that are reproductively isolated from other such groups."
A species may have subgroups, called subspecies or varieties, showing certain distinct features of their own.

questions.question: Systema Naturae is a book written by:
questions.question_raw: Systema Naturae is a book written by:
questions.explanation: ‘Systema Naturae’ is a book written by Carolus Linneaus. This book gives in-depth details of the classification system and binomial nomenclature of organisms.

questions.question: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.question_raw: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.explanation: Systematics is the scientific study of the diversity of organisms and their evolutionary relationship. Systematics is also known as taxonomy. It includes identification, nomenclature, and classification.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Full form of ICBN is:
questions.question_raw: Full form of ICBN is:
questions.explanation: ICBN stands for the International code of botanical nomenclature that frames rules for the nomenclature of plants applicable all over the world.

questions.question: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.question_raw: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.explanation: Species is the lowest rank in the hierarchy. It means species contain the least general characters as compared to other categories.

questions.question: Identify from the following, category which has real existence.
questions.question_raw: Identify from the following, category which has real existence.
questions.explanation: Species

questions.question: Classification based on the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called:
questions.question_raw: Classification based on the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called:
questions.explanation: The classification of organisms on the basis of the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called a phylogenetic system of classification. The organisms are arranged according to their evolution on earth.

questions.question: The branch of study that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The branch of study that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification is called _______.
questions.explanation: Taxonomy is the branch of biological science that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification of organisms according to certain sets of rules.

questions.question: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.question_raw: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.explanation: A genus consists of a large number of closed related species. Genus Solanum consist of larger common species that include potato, tomato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: Living organisms are classified because of:
questions.question_raw: Living organisms are classified because of:

options.label: B
options.text: Easy study and to keep records

options.label: C
options.text: To bring out similarities and to know habitat

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with:
questions.question_raw: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with:
questions.explanation: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with Binomial nomenclature in which the name of the organism consists of the genus name and species name.

questions.question: Which one is an obligate category of classification?
questions.question_raw: Which one is an obligate category of classification?
questions.explanation: Family is the obligate category of classification as it forms the basic functional unit of classification. The family includes all the related species having similar characters.

questions.question: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.question_raw: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.explanation: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in museums. Hence, the correct option is the Museum.

questions.question: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.question_raw: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.explanation: Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone's taxon. Hence, the correct option is Herbarium.

questions.question: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in ______.
questions.question_raw: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in ______.
questions.explanation: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in the museum.

questions.question: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.question_raw: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.explanation: The parks and zoo are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Biodiversity.

questions.question: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in ______.
questions.question_raw: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in ______.
questions.explanation: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in the museum. Hence, the correct choice is the Museum.

questions.question: The famous botanical garden, Kew is located in ______.
questions.question_raw: The famous botanical garden, Kew is located in ______.
questions.explanation: Kew Gardens is a botanical garden in southwest London that houses the "largest and most diverse botanical and mycological collections in the world".

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Herbarium is:
questions.question_raw: Herbarium is:

options.label: B
options.text: Collection of living plants for reference

options.label: D
options.text: Collection of preserved plant and animal specimen

questions.explanation: The herbarium is a storehouse of collected plant specimens that are dried, pressed and preserved on sheets. Hence, the correct option is Collection of dried plant specimens.

questions.question: National Botanical Research Institute is situated at ______.
questions.question_raw: National Botanical Research Institute is situated at ______.
questions.explanation: The famous botanical gardens are at Kew (England), Indian Botanical Garden, Howrah (India) and at National Botanical Research Institute, Lucknow (India). Hence, the correct choice is Lucknow.

questions.question: It is a handy book containing instructions of occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area that is known as ________.
questions.question_raw: It is a handy book containing instructions of occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area that is known as ________.
questions.explanation: Flora, manuals, monographs and catalogues are different means of recording descriptions. Manuals are useful in providing information for identification of names of species found in an area. Hence, the correct option is Manual.

questions.question: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.question_raw: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.explanation: Zoo and parks are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Key.

questions.question: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.question_raw: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.explanation: The keys are based on the contrasting characters generally in a pair called a couplet. Manuals are useful in providing information for identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Hence, the correct option is Lead.

questions.question: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.question_raw: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.explanation: Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Catalogue is also another means of recording descriptions which helps incorrect identification. Hence, the correct option is Flora.

questions.question: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.question_raw: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.explanation: The national botanical research institute is located in Lucknow (UP). This is one of the largest botanical research institutes in the world housing thousands of plants.

questions.question: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.question_raw: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.explanation: Rajaji national park is an Indian national park and tiger reserve that encompasses the Shivaliks, near the foothill of the Himalayas in Uttrakhand.

questions.question: Lions are found in:
questions.question_raw: Lions are found in:
questions.explanation: In India, lions are found in Gir forests located in the state of Gujarat, India. It is the sole home of the Asiatic lions and is considered to be one of the most important protected areas in Asia.

questions.question: Largest Botanical Garden in the world is:
questions.question_raw: Largest Botanical Garden in the world is:
questions.explanation:
The Royal Botanical Garden, Kew (located in London, United Kingdom) is the largest and most renowned botanical garden in the world. It covers approximately 330 acres and houses one of the most extensive and diverse collections of living plants and preserved plant specimens. Kew Gardens is also a leading center for botanical research and conservation.
It is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a leading center for botanical research and conservation.

questions.question: List of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified is______.
questions.question_raw: List of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified is______.
questions.explanation: Key is the list of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified. It is an important tool for the classification of the organism.

questions.question: The first Biosphere Reserve established in India is:
questions.question_raw: The first Biosphere Reserve established in India is:
questions.explanation: The Nilgiri Biosphere reserve is the first biosphere reserve established in India. Biosphere reserves are set to protect the flora and fauna of a particular area that houses a large number of wild animals.

questions.question: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.explanation: A biosphere reserve is divided into a number of zones, where different kinds of activities are allowed. In the outer zone, agriculture and other activities are allowed but in the central or core zone, no human activities are allowed.

questions.question: Preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs is
questions.question_raw: Preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs is
questions.explanation: In a biological laboratory, formalin is used as a preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs.

questions.question: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.question_raw: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.explanation: Manual is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area. It serves as an important tool for the classification of organisms.

chapters.name: Biological Classification
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

questions.question: The five kingdom classification was proposed by
questions.question_raw: The five kingdom classification was proposed by
questions.explanation: Five kingdom classification was proposed by R.H. Whittaker in 1969. The kingdoms identified by him were Monera, Protista,Fungi, Plantae and Animalia.

questions.question: Which among the five-kingdom system of classification do not possess nuclear membrane?
questions.question_raw: Which among the five-kingdom system of classification do not possess nuclear membrane?
questions.explanation: Kingdom Protista, kingdom Fungi, kingdom Plantae possess the nuclear membrane while the kingdom Monera does not possess a nuclear membrane.

questions.question: In five kingdom of classification, Whittaker assigned eukaryotes to how many kingdoms?
questions.question_raw: In five kingdom of classification, Whittaker assigned eukaryotes to how many kingdoms?
questions.explanation: In Whittaker’s five kingdoms of classification, kingdom Protista, kingdom fungi, kingdom Plantae and kingdom Animalia are eukaryotic organisms. The kingdom monera includes prokaryotic organisms. Hence, four kingdoms out of five are eukayotes.

questions.question: In five kingdom of classification, prokaryotic, non-nucleated and unicellular organisms are included under:
questions.question_raw: In five kingdom of classification, prokaryotic, non-nucleated and unicellular organisms are included under:
questions.explanation: The fifth kingdom, Monera, contains all the single-celled organisms that do not possess a true nucleus. Members of the kingdom Monera are commonly referred to as bacteria.

questions.question: Which kingdom brought Chlorella and Chlamydomonas with Paramoecium and Amoeba?
questions.question_raw: Which kingdom brought Chlorella and Chlamydomonas with Paramoecium and Amoeba?
questions.explanation: Kingdom Protista has brought together Chlamydomonas, Chlorella which were earlier placed in Algae within Plants and (both having cell walls) with Paramoecium and Amoeba which was earlier placed in the animal kingdom which lacks it.

questions.question: In five kingdom of classification, the kingdom Plantae are autotrophic, eukaryotic and_______.
questions.question_raw: In five kingdom of classification, the kingdom Plantae are autotrophic, eukaryotic and_______.
questions.explanation: The Kingdom Plantae includes all types of eukaryotic, multicellular, photosynthetic plants found in this biosphere. Most of the organism in this kingdom are autotrophs, which synthesis their own food with the help of solar energy.

questions.question: Who was the earliest to attempt a more scientific basis for classification?
questions.question_raw: Who was the earliest to attempt a more scientific basis for classification?
questions.explanation: Aristotle was the earliest to attempt a more scientific basis for classification. He used simple morphological characters to classify plants into trees, shrubs, and herbs.

questions.question: Who divided animals into two groups based on the presence and absence of red blood cells?
questions.question_raw: Who divided animals into two groups based on the presence and absence of red blood cells?
questions.explanation: Aristotle used simple morphological characters to classify plants into trees, shrubs, and herbs. He also divided animals into two groups, those which had red blood and those that did not.

questions.question: Which system of classification was developed during Linnaeus's time?
questions.question_raw: Which system of classification was developed during Linnaeus's time?
questions.explanation: In Linnaeus' time, a two Kingdom system of classification with Plantae and Animalia kingdoms was developed that included all plants and animals respectively. This system was used until very recently.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms were placed together under algae?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following organisms were placed together under algae?
questions.explanation: As spirogyra, chlorella and chlamydomonas have same characterstics which are perfectly suitable for the 'algae', they all are placed together in algae.

questions.question: Who is called as Father of taxonomy?
questions.question_raw: Who is called as Father of taxonomy?
questions.explanation: Carl Linnaeus, also known as Carl von Linné or Carolus Linnaeus, is often called the Father of Taxonomy. His system for naming, ranking, and classifying organisms is still in wide use today with many changes.

questions.question: Which of the following type of classification based on a few morphological characters?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following type of classification based on a few morphological characters?
questions.explanation: Artificial classification is based on a few morphological characters of organisms like flower shape, the stem type and types of seeds.

questions.question: The first phylogenetic system of classification was given by:
questions.question_raw: The first phylogenetic system of classification was given by:
questions.explanation: The first phylogenetic system of classification was given by Engler and Prantl which was based on the evolutionary relationship of the organisms. Those evolved earlier are classified first and newly evolved later on.

questions.question: Classification and studies based on chemical constituents of an organism are called _______.
questions.question_raw: Classification and studies based on chemical constituents of an organism are called _______.
questions.explanation: Chemotaxonomy as the method of biological classification based on similarities in the structure of certain compounds among the organisms being classified. Two organisms morphologically similar to each other are classified on the basis of chemotaxonomy.

questions.question: Which one is not a characteristic feature of prokaryotes?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a characteristic feature of prokaryotes?

options.label: C
options.text: Genetic material not organized in nucleus

questions.explanation: Prokaryotic organisms generally do not reproduce by sexual reproduction. However, they reproduce by asexual methods in favorable conditions.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

questions.question: Arranging organism on the basis of their shared similar or derived characters that differ from ancestral character is called _________.
questions.question_raw: Arranging organism on the basis of their shared similar or derived characters that differ from ancestral character is called _________.
questions.explanation: A cladogram is arranging organisms on the basis of their shared similar or derived characters that differ from ancestral character. It is an important tool for taxonomical studies.

questions.question: Unicellular algae and fungi are separated from other organisms on the basis of:
questions.question_raw: Unicellular algae and fungi are separated from other organisms on the basis of:
questions.explanation: Unicellular organisms are made of only one cell and there is an absence of tissue differentiation. All unicellular algae and fungi are separated from other organisms on this basis.

questions.question: Prokaryotes differ from Eukaryotes in the absence of:
questions.question_raw: Prokaryotes differ from Eukaryotes in the absence of:

options.label: A
options.text: Nuclear membrane and membrane bounded organelles

correct_option_texts: Nuclear membrane and membrane bounded organelles
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes differ from Eukaryotes in absence of nuclear membrane and membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, Golgi bodies, etc.

questions.question: Classification based on comparative cytological studies, the number of chromosome and behaviour of chromosome is called _______.
questions.question_raw: Classification based on comparative cytological studies, the number of chromosome and behaviour of chromosome is called _______.
questions.explanation: Cytotaxonomy is the branch of biology dealing with the relationships and classification of organisms using comparative studies of chromosomes.

questions.question: Plant cells differ from animal cells in:
questions.question_raw: Plant cells differ from animal cells in:

options.label: A
options.text: Having cell membrane and Golgi bodies

questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall made up of cellulose and chloroplast that contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis. Animal cell lacks both cell wall and chloroplast.

questions.question: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in case of
questions.question_raw: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in case of
questions.explanation: Algae may be unicellular as well as multicellular, primitive algae are unicellular, and red and green algae are multicellular having a length of several meters. It is not possible to distinguish unicellular and multicellular algae because of this that unicellular green algae have not been included in kingdom Protista by Whittaker.

questions.question: Which of the following archaebacteria is present in the guts of ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following archaebacteria is present in the guts of ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes?
questions.explanation: Methanogens bacteria are present in the guts of ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes. The characteristic smell of cow dungs is due to methane gas produced by this bacteria.

questions.question: A peculiar odour found in marshy areas and cow sheds is of gas produced by________.
questions.question_raw: A peculiar odour found in marshy areas and cow sheds is of gas produced by________.
questions.explanation: The peculiar odour found in marshy areas and cow sheds is methane gas produced by methanogens bacteria that belong to class Archaebacteria.

questions.question: The unicellular eukaryotes belong to the kingdom______.
questions.question_raw: The unicellular eukaryotes belong to the kingdom______.
questions.explanation: Unicellular Eukaryotes were included in the kingdom Protista while unicellular Prokaryotes were included in the kingdom Monera.

questions.question: A kingdom common to unicellular plants and animals is_______.
questions.question_raw: A kingdom common to unicellular plants and animals is_______.
questions.explanation: Protista includes all unicellular prokaryotic organisms that include both plants and animals. So, Protista is common to unicellular plants and animals.

questions.question: The infectious spore-like stage is seen in_______.
questions.question_raw: The infectious spore-like stage is seen in_______.
questions.explanation: Sporozoans include diverse organisms that have an infectious spore-like stage in their life cycle.

questions.question: In which group of kingdom Protista, plasmodium is formed?
questions.question_raw: In which group of kingdom Protista, plasmodium is formed?
questions.explanation: Slime moulds are saprophytic protists. The body moves along decaying twigs and leaves engulfing organic material. Under suitable conditions, they form an aggregation called plasmodium which may grow and spread over several feet. During unfavourable conditions, the plasmodium differentiates and forms fruiting bodies bearing spores at their tips.

questions.question: Which criterion made Fungi to be placed in separate groups?
questions.question_raw: Which criterion made Fungi to be placed in separate groups?
questions.explanation: The cell wall of fungi is made up of chitin while the green plants had a cellulosic cell wall. Due to this, fungi were placed in a separate kingdom – Kingdom Fungi.

questions.question: How bacteria reproduce during unfavourable conditions?
questions.question_raw: How bacteria reproduce during unfavourable conditions?
questions.explanation: Bacteria reproduce mainly by fission. Sometimes, under unfavourable conditions, they produce spores. They also reproduce by a sort of sexual reproduction by adopting a primitive type of DNA transfer from one bacterium to the other.

questions.question: The filamentous body of the fungus is called _________.
questions.question_raw: The filamentous body of the fungus is called _________.
questions.explanation: Mycelium is the vegetative part of a fungus or fungus-like bacterial colony, consisting of a mass of branching, thread-like hyphae.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

questions.question: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included under the kingdom:
questions.question_raw: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included under the kingdom:
questions.explanation: All prokaryotic, unicellular organisms are included in the kingdom Monera. The Eukaryotic unicellular organisms are placed in the Protista kingdom.

questions.question: The cell wall of fungi consists of _______.
questions.question_raw: The cell wall of fungi consists of _______.
questions.explanation: The cell wall of fungi consists of special sugar molecules called chitin. The cell wall of plants is made up of cellulose.

questions.question: Which among the following is photosynthetic as well as saprophytic?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is photosynthetic as well as saprophytic?
questions.explanation: Euglena is photosynthetic in presence of sunlight and is saprophytic in absence of sunlight.

questions.question: Which bacteria help in oxidising inorganic substances?
questions.question_raw: Which bacteria help in oxidising inorganic substances?
questions.explanation: Chemosynthetic autotrophic bacteria oxidize various inorganic substances such as nitrates, nitrites, and ammonia and use the released energy for their ATP production. They play a great role in recycling nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorous, iron, and sulphur.

questions.question: The asexual reproduction in fungi takes place by
questions.question_raw: The asexual reproduction in fungi takes place by
questions.explanation: In fungi, the asexual reproduction takes place by spores called conidia or sporangiospores or zoospores, and sexual reproduction is by oospores, ascospores and basidiospores.

questions.question: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in________.
questions.question_raw: A distinction between unicellular and multicellular is not possible in________.
questions.explanation: Algae grouped together the unicellular organisms and the multicellular ones.

questions.question: Which among the following is not included in the three domains system?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is not included in the three domains system?
questions.explanation: The three-domain system includes Archaebacteria, Eubacteria, and Bacteria domain.

questions.question: Which archaebacteria is present in guts of ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes?
questions.question_raw: Which archaebacteria is present in guts of ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes?
questions.explanation: Methanogens are present in the guts of several ruminant animals such as cows and buffaloes and they are responsible for the production of methane (biogas) from the dung of these animals.

questions.question: The blue-green algae are also referred to as________.
questions.question_raw: The blue-green algae are also referred to as________.
questions.explanation: Blue-green algae are also called cyanobacteria. They are considered as the most primitive organism that still survives. It includes different forms of unicellular and multicellular organisms.

questions.question: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell known that can highly pleomorphic is _________.
questions.question_raw: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell known that can highly pleomorphic is _________.
questions.explanation:
Mycoplasma is a genus of bacteria that lack a cell wall around their cell membrane. Without a cell wall, they are unaffected by many common antibiotics such as penicillin or other beta-lactam antibiotics that target cell wall synthesis.
Mycoplasma species are the smallest bacterial cells yet discovered, can survive without oxygen, and come in various shapes. For example, M. genitalium is flask-shaped.

questions.question: Which one is not a member of the kingdom Protista?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a member of the kingdom Protista?
questions.explanation: Phycomycetes is a subdivision of fungus which are multicellular. Protozoa, slime moulds and euglenoids are part of the kingdom Protista which are unicellular and eukaryotic organisms.

questions.question: The class of fungi in which mushroom is included is________.
questions.question_raw: The class of fungi in which mushroom is included is________.
questions.explanation: Commonly known forms of basidiomycetes are mushrooms, bracket fungi, or puffballs.

questions.question: Diatoms do not decay easily because:
questions.question_raw: Diatoms do not decay easily because:

options.label: B
options.text: Their body is impervious to water

options.label: D
options.text: They are abundant in saline water

questions.explanation: The walls of the diatoms are embedded with silica and thus the walls are indestructible.

questions.question: In fungi like ascomycetes and basidiomycetes, an intervening dikaryotic stage occurs. Such condition is called
questions.question_raw: In fungi like ascomycetes and basidiomycetes, an intervening dikaryotic stage occurs. Such condition is called
questions.explanation: In fungi like ascomycetes and basidiomycetes, an intervening dikaryotic stage (n + n i.e. two nuclei per cell) occurs; such a condition is called a dikaryon and the phase is called dikaryon phase of fungus.

questions.question: The body of the fungus is filamentous and it is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The body of the fungus is filamentous and it is called _______.
questions.explanation: The body of the filamentous fungus is known as mycelium, which is a thread-like structure. Mycelium produces hyphae and sporogenous cells to produce spores.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

questions.question: Cellular genes which are known to take part in producing cancer are termed as
questions.question_raw: Cellular genes which are known to take part in producing cancer are termed as
questions.explanation: Proto-oncogenes are a group of genes that cause normal cells to become cancerous when they are mutated.

questions.question: Which one is important in nutrient to recycle and act as decomposer and mineralizes of the biosphere ______.
questions.question_raw: Which one is important in nutrient to recycle and act as decomposer and mineralizes of the biosphere ______.
questions.explanation: Fungi is important in the nutrient cycle and acts as decomposer along with bacteria and mineralizers of the biosphere to regular recycling of nutrients in the biosphere.

questions.question: In fungus, food reserves are:
questions.question_raw: In fungus, food reserves are:
questions.explanation: Different fungi contain different kinds of reserve food materials depending upon the habitat and body structure of the fungus. In some fungi, the food reserves are glycogen and fat.

questions.question: In which class of fungi the mushroom belongs to:
questions.question_raw: In which class of fungi the mushroom belongs to:
questions.explanation: The mushroom belongs to class Basidiomycetes to kingdom fungi. Basidiomycetes produce basidiospores as reproductive units. The member of this group grows on dead and decaying matters.

questions.question: Which is the possible region of respiration in bacteria?
questions.question_raw: Which is the possible region of respiration in bacteria?
questions.explanation: Bacteria belong to the kingdom Monera in which organisms are unicellular and prokaryotic. Prokaryotic organisms lack membrane-bounded organelles like mitochondria where respiration occurs. In bacteria, respiration occurs in the mesosome.

questions.question: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals is probably having_____.
questions.question_raw: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals is probably having_____.
questions.explanation: A bacterium capable of tolerating extreme heat, dryness, and toxic chemicals have the ability to produce endospores inside the body that help them to tide over unfavorable conditions.

questions.question: When a freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water:
questions.question_raw: When a freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water:

options.label: B
options.text: The contractile vacuole increase in size

questions.explanation: When freshwater protozoan is placed in marine water, the contractile vacuole disappears because the function of the vacuole is to expel out excess water but in the case of marine water, the water should be retained instead of getting expelled. If the contractile vacuole will not disappear the cell will get dehydrated.

questions.question: The plant cells differ from animal cells in:
questions.question_raw: The plant cells differ from animal cells in:
questions.explanation: The plant cells have a eukaryotic structure with prominent chloroplasts and cell walls mainly made of cellulose.

questions.question: Plants complete their life cycle in two phase, sporophytic and gametophytic which occurs alternately called
questions.question_raw: Plants complete their life cycle in two phase, sporophytic and gametophytic which occurs alternately called
questions.explanation: The fluctuation between these diploid and haploid stages that occurs in plants is called the alternation of generations. The multicellular haploid plant structure is called the gametophyte, which is formed from the spore and gives rise to the haploid gametes.

questions.question: Sexual reproduction is by the population of male and female followed by embryological development is a characteristic feature of_______.
questions.question_raw: Sexual reproduction is by the population of male and female followed by embryological development is a characteristic feature of_______.
questions.explanation: The sexual reproduction in the kingdom Animalia is by the population of males and female followed by embryological development.

questions.question: A plant that is partially heterotrophic is______.
questions.question_raw: A plant that is partially heterotrophic is______.
questions.explanation: Drosera plant is partially heterotrophic. These plants capture insects to fulfil its protein requirement although it performs photosynthesis also.

questions.question: Who demonstrated that the extract of the infected plants of tobacco could cause infection in healthy plants?
questions.question_raw: Who demonstrated that the extract of the infected plants of tobacco could cause infection in healthy plants?
questions.explanation: Beijerinck is considered one of the founders of virology. In 1898, he published results on the filtration experiments demonstrating that tobacco mosaic disease is caused by an infectious agent smaller than a bacterium and called the fluid as Contagiumvivum fluidum (infectious living fluid).

questions.question: The outer protein covering of virus is_______.
questions.question_raw: The outer protein covering of virus is_______.
questions.explanation: A capsid is the protein shell of a virus. It consists of several oligomeric structural subunits made of a protein called protomers. The capsid encloses the genetic material of the virus.

questions.question: The genetic material of a virus is:
questions.question_raw: The genetic material of a virus is:
questions.explanation: Most viruses have either RNA or DNA as their genetic material. The nucleic acid may be single- or double-stranded. The entire infectious virus particle, called a virion, consists of the nucleic acid and an outer shell of protein.

questions.question: Diener discovered_____.
questions.question_raw: Diener discovered_____.
questions.explanation: T.O. Diener discovered a new infectious agent that was smaller than viruses and caused potato spindle tuber disease. It was found to be a free RNA. It lacked the protein coat that is found in viruses, hence the name viroid.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Biological Classification

questions.question: A peculiar group of the dual organism which is formed by an association between algae and a fungus.
questions.question_raw: A peculiar group of the dual organism which is formed by an association between algae and a fungus.
questions.explanation: A lichen is a composite organism that arises from algae or cyanobacteria living among filaments of multiple fungi in a symbiotic relationship. The combined lichen has properties different from those of its component organisms.

questions.question: A virus that infects and replicates within a bacterium is called
questions.question_raw: A virus that infects and replicates within a bacterium is called
questions.explanation: A bacteriophage is a virus that infects and replicates within a bacterium.

questions.question: Which is NOT the characteristic of viroids?
questions.question_raw: Which is NOT the characteristic of viroids?

options.label: A
options.text: The RNA of the viroid has low molecular weight

options.label: D
options.text: It caused potato spindle tuber disease

questions.explanation: Viroids are smaller than viruses and caused potato spindle tuber disease. It was found to be a free RNA; it lacked the protein coat that is found in viruses, hence the name viroid. The RNA of the viroid was of low molecular weight.

questions.question: The name virus was first given by_______.
questions.question_raw: The name virus was first given by_______.
questions.explanation: The name virus was first given by Beijerinck, in 1898.

questions.question: Identify the virus in figure given below
questions.question_raw: Identify the virus in figure given below
questions.explanation: A bacteriophage is a virus that infects and replicates within a bacterium.Bacteriophages are composed of proteins that encapsulate a DNA or RNA genome, and may have relatively simple or elaborate structures.

questions.question: The genetic material for retrovirus is_________.
questions.question_raw: The genetic material for retrovirus is_________.
questions.explanation: The virus in which genetic material is RNA is called a retrovirus. In this virus reverse transcription takes place to form DNA that synthesis proteins.

questions.question: The three domains system do not include:
questions.question_raw: The three domains system do not include:
questions.explanation: Three kingdoms or domain system of classification does not include the viral domain. The virus was discovered after the invention of the electron microscope so, they were not part of the living world.

questions.question: Identify the cuboidal virus from the following:
questions.question_raw: Identify the cuboidal virus from the following:
questions.explanation: A virus consists of genetic material (DNA or RNA) surrounded by protein capsules. They are of different shapes and sizes. The poliomyelitis virus is cubical in shape.

questions.question: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell know can survive without oxygen is_______.
questions.question_raw: The organism that completely lacks a cell wall and is the smallest living cell know can survive without oxygen is_______.
questions.explanation: Mycoplasmas are a group of organisms that lack a cell wall and are the smallest living organisms. They can survive without oxygen. Mycoplasmas are also called as PPLO (pleuro pneumonia-like organism).

questions.question: Which one can be photosynthetic as well as saprophytic?
questions.question_raw: Which one can be photosynthetic as well as saprophytic?
questions.explanation: Euglena is a photosynthetic as well as a saprophytic organism. It contains chlorophyll for photosynthesis and can absorb food from dead and decaying materials.

questions.question: Select the incorrect pair:
questions.question_raw: Select the incorrect pair:
questions.explanation: Mycophages are not a group of bacteria. They are a group of organisms that use algae as food. The animal virus is called Zoophagineae, plant virus phytophagineae, and the bacterial virus is called bacteriophages.

questions.question: Two categories of animals on the basis of red blood are:
questions.question_raw: Two categories of animals on the basis of red blood are:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin in RBCs imparts red colour to it. The organisms whose blood contains haemoglobin are called enaima and those without it are called anaima.

questions.question: The fusion of protoplasm between to motile or non-motile gamete is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fusion of protoplasm between to motile or non-motile gamete is called ________.
questions.explanation: Plasmogamy is the fusion of protoplasm of two cells which may be motile or non- motile. Plasmogamy occurs during sexual reproduction to form a diploid organism.

questions.question: Plant disease in which pathogen is seen as cottony growth on the surface of the host is:
questions.question_raw: Plant disease in which pathogen is seen as cottony growth on the surface of the host is:
questions.explanation: Downy mildew disease is caused due to fungus that forms cottony growth on the surface of the host. The leaves' surface gets reduced that alters the photosynthesis rate.

chapters.name: Plant Kingdom
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

questions.question: In Phaeophyceae, the food stored in the form of:
questions.question_raw: In Phaeophyceae, the food stored in the form of:
questions.explanation: In Phaeophyceae, food is stored as complex carbohydrates which may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol.

questions.question: Kelps are ________.
questions.question_raw: Kelps are ________.
questions.explanation: Kelps are marine algae of class Phaeophyceae or brown algae. The body of kelp may reach up to 100 meters long. They vary in colour from olive green to various shades of brown depending upon xanthophyll pigments, fucoxanthin present in them.

questions.question: An algae having ribbon-shaped chloroplast is_______.
questions.question_raw: An algae having ribbon-shaped chloroplast is_______.
questions.explanation: The shape of a chloroplast varies from species to species. It may be cup-shaped (e.g., Chlamydomonas), (e.g., Vaucheria), Girdle (e.g., Ulothrix), Stellate or Star-shaped (e.g., Zygnema), Reticulate or net-like (e.g., Cladophora, Oedogonium), Spiral or ribbon or scalariform (e.g., Spirogyra), ovoid or disc or spheroid in higher plants.

questions.question: Laminaria and Fucus belong to:
questions.question_raw: Laminaria and Fucus belong to:
questions.explanation: The common forms of Brown algae or Phaeophyceae are Ectocarpus, Dictyota, Laminaria, Sargassum and Fucus.

questions.question: Angiosperm: roots :: Phaeophyceae:
questions.question_raw: Angiosperm: roots :: Phaeophyceae:
questions.explanation: The plant body of Phaeophyceae is usually attached to the substratum by a holdfast whereas the angiosperms attach to the substratum with the help of roots.

questions.question: Half of the total carbon dioxide fixation on earth is carried out by algae through ________.
questions.question_raw: Half of the total carbon dioxide fixation on earth is carried out by algae through ________.
questions.explanation: Being photosynthetic, algae increase the level of dissolved oxygen in their immediate environment.

questions.question: In Ulothrix or Spirogyra, reduction division (meiosis) occurs at the time of:
questions.question_raw: In Ulothrix or Spirogyra, reduction division (meiosis) occurs at the time of:
questions.explanation: In Ulothrix/Spirogyra reduction division (meiosis) occurs at the time of zygospore germination. The plant body of Ulothrix and Spirogyra is gametophytic (haploid), they produce zoogametes (n) which fuses to form zygospores (2n) diploid, which is a resting spore. The onset of favourable condition zygospore undergoes reductional division, or meiosis to produce zoo- meiospores.

questions.question: The cell wall of green algae is made up of _______.
questions.question_raw: The cell wall of green algae is made up of _______.
questions.explanation: Green algae usually have a rigid cell wall made of an inner layer of cellulose and an outer layer of pectose.

questions.question: Which classes of algae possess chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids and xanthophylls?
questions.question_raw: Which classes of algae possess chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids and xanthophylls?
questions.explanation: The class Chlorophyceae (Green Algae) contains chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, carotenoids, and xanthophylls as their primary pigments. These pigments give them a distinct green color and help in photosynthesis.

questions.question: The process of fusion between one large and non-motile female gamete with smaller, motile male gamete is termed as________.
questions.question_raw: The process of fusion between one large and non-motile female gamete with smaller, motile male gamete is termed as________.
questions.explanation: The fusion between one large, non-motile female gamete and a smaller, motile male gamete is termed as oogamous e.g., Volvox, Fucus.

questions.question: Rhodophyta is commonly called red algae because of the predominance of pigment________.
questions.question_raw: Rhodophyta is commonly called red algae because of the predominance of pigment________.
questions.explanation: The algal class Rhodophyta are commonly called red algae because it contains a red pigment, r-phycoerythrin along with chlorophyll a and d.

questions.question: Agar is obtained from_______.
questions.question_raw: Agar is obtained from_______.
questions.explanation: Agar, one of the commercial products obtained from Gelidium are used to grow microbes and in preparations of ice-creams and jellies.

questions.question: The largest unicellular organism is_________.
questions.question_raw: The largest unicellular organism is_________.
questions.explanation: Acetabularia is a genus of green algae, especially of the Polyphyaceae family. It is the largest unicellular organism.

questions.question: Pond scum is________.
questions.question_raw: Pond scum is________.
questions.explanation: The green patchy structure that develops around the surface of stagnant water bodies having thread-like mass is called pond scum. They are actually a species of green algae, Spirogyra.

questions.question: Algae with ribbon-shaped chloroplast _______.
questions.question_raw: Algae with ribbon-shaped chloroplast _______.
questions.explanation: Spirogyra is an alga that has a long thread-like structure having a ribbon-shaped chloroplast. Spirogyra is found in stagnant water forming a green layer around the surface called pond scum.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

questions.question: The algae that secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls to contribute to producing coral reefs is______.
questions.question_raw: The algae that secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls to contribute to producing coral reefs is______.
questions.explanation: Red algae in the order Corallinales, characterized by a thallus that is hard because of calcareous deposits which contribute to producing coral reefs in seawater.

questions.question: Identify the algae present in the figure
questions.question_raw: Identify the algae present in the figure
questions.explanation: The algae present in the figure is Chlamydomonas which is a unicellular algae present in fresh water. Two flagella is attached to it that helps in the movement in water.

questions.question: Which of the following is responsible for peat formation ?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is responsible for peat formation ?
questions.explanation: Sphagnum mosses not only dominate large areas of the boreal peatlands, but they have also formed these habitats by their growth and production of decay-resistant litter. As they invade, they can quickly transform a high-pH fen with little peat accumulation to a bog where large amounts of carbon are stored. They are also responsible for the many types of surface patterns observed in peatlands. Their ability to shape and transform mires can to a large extent be understood from the peculiar anatomy and biochemistry, a fact which makes the links between species’ traits and ecosystem functions in peatlands very strong.

questions.question: Which of the following is produced by moss spore on germination?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is produced by moss spore on germination?
questions.explanation: The spore of moss germinate to produce a protonema, which is a green, branched, and frequently filamentous stage and develops a lateral bud which is called secondary protonema or a leafy stage. Secondary protonema also gives rise to gametophore initials. Secondary protonema can undergo both sexual and asexual modes of reproduction. It reproduces asexually by the processes of budding and fragmentation. Sexual reproduction is the process that occurs in the leafy stage.

questions.question: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by specialised structures, commonly known as:
questions.question_raw: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by specialised structures, commonly known as:
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction in liverworts takes place by the formation of specialized structures called gemmae (sing. gemma). Gemmae are green, multicellular, asexual buds, which develop in small receptacles called gemma cups located on the thalli. The gemmae become detached from the parent body and germinate to form new individuals.

questions.question: A plant producing seeds but lacking flowers is:
questions.question_raw: A plant producing seeds but lacking flowers is:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms produce seeds without the formation of flowers. The gymnosperms (Gymnos: naked, Sperma: seeds) are planted in which the ovules are not enclosed by any ovary wall and remain exposed, both before and after fertilization. The seeds that develop post-fertilization, are not covered, i.e., are naked.

questions.question: A moss plant used as a fuel is __________.
questions.question_raw: A moss plant used as a fuel is __________.
questions.explanation: Species of Sphagnum, a moss, provide peat that has long been used as fuel.

questions.question: Which of the following is also called 'amphibians of plant kingdom'?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is also called 'amphibians of plant kingdom'?
questions.explanation: Bryophytes are also called amphibians of the plant kingdom because these plants can live in soil but are dependent on water for sexual reproduction as they release antherozoids into the water where they come in contact with archegonium to produce a zygote.

questions.question: Bryophytes flourish is moist and shady environments because:
questions.question_raw: Bryophytes flourish is moist and shady environments because:
questions.explanation: These plants can live in soil but are dependent on water for sexual reproduction as they release antherozoids into the water where they come in contact with archegonium to produce a zygote. They usually occur in damp, humid, and shaded localities.

questions.question: Archegoniophore is found in ________.
questions.question_raw: Archegoniophore is found in ________.
questions.explanation: Archegoniophore (female gametophytes ) develop on the thallus of Marchantia. Each archegonium produces a single egg cell which helps in sexual reproduction.

questions.question: The root-like structure which helps in anchorage and absorption of water in Bryophytes is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The root-like structure which helps in anchorage and absorption of water in Bryophytes is called ________.
questions.explanation: Rhizoids are the root-like structures present in Bryophytes that help in anchorage and absorption of water. They are thin hair-like structures generally present at the ventral surface of Bryophytes.

questions.question: Which of the following functions is carried out by Moss peristome?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following functions is carried out by Moss peristome?
questions.explanation: All moss capsules have an operculum and peristome. During spore dispersal, peristome teeth bend outwards and spores fall out of the capsule and are carried by air currents.

questions.question: Thallus of Riccia is _________.
questions.question_raw: Thallus of Riccia is _________.
questions.explanation: Riccia is a genus of liverworts in the order Marchantiales. In bryophytes, the plant body is generally haploid (n) that alternate with a diploid form for shorter period. The thallus of Riccia is Haploid.

questions.question: Which one is peat moss?
questions.question_raw: Which one is peat moss?
questions.explanation: Sphagnum is also known as peat moss as it provides peat that has long been used as fuel.

questions.question: Both Pteridophytes and Gymnosperms possesses_________.
questions.question_raw: Both Pteridophytes and Gymnosperms possesses_________.
questions.explanation: Pteridophytes and Gymnosperms belong to a different division of plant kingdom but both of them possess Archegonia as female sex organ which produces female gamete for sexual reproduction.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

questions.question: Cycas is a gymnosperm as it has:
questions.question_raw: Cycas is a gymnosperm as it has:
questions.explanation: The gymnosperms (Gymnos: naked, Sperma: seeds) are plants in which the ovules are not enclosed by any ovary wall and remain exposed, both before and after fertilization. The seeds that develop post-fertilization, are not covered, i.e., are naked. Since Cycas has naked seeds, it belongs to gymnosperms.

questions.question: In which pteridophytes, heterosporous is produced?
questions.question_raw: In which pteridophytes, heterosporous is produced?
questions.explanation: Genera like Selaginella and Salvinia which produce two kinds of spores, macro (large) and micro (small) spores are known as heterosporous.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out among the following: Dryopteris, Pteris, Adiantum, Selaginella.
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one out among the following: Dryopteris, Pteris, Adiantum, Selaginella.
questions.explanation: Selaginella belongs to class Lycopsida and other plants (Dryopteris, Pteris, Adiantum) belong to class Pteropsida of Pteridophytes.

questions.question: The giant redwood tree is ________.
questions.question_raw: The giant redwood tree is ________.
questions.explanation: One of the gymnosperms, Giant sequoias are the world's largest single tree with an average height of 50 - 85 m (164 - 279 ft) and 6 - 8 m (20 - 26 ft) in diameter.

questions.question: Which among the following belong to Sphenopsida?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following belong to Sphenopsida?
questions.explanation: Equisetum of Pteridophyta belongs to class Sphenopsida.

questions.question: Identify the plant shown in the figure given below.
questions.question_raw: Identify the plant shown in the figure given below.
questions.explanation: The plant shown in the figure is cycas that belongs to division Cycadophyta and family Cycadaceae. It is dioecious and Cycas circinalis species is endemic to India.

questions.question: Male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence in_____.
questions.question_raw: Male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence in_____.
questions.explanation: Unlike bryophytes and pteridophytes, in gymnosperms (Cedrus) the male and the female gametophytes do not have an independent free-living existence.

questions.question: The structure formed by germinated fern spore is:
questions.question_raw: The structure formed by germinated fern spore is:
questions.explanation: Fern spores germinate to give rise to inconspicuous, small but multicellular, free-living, mostly photosynthetic thalloid gametophytes called prothallus. The gametophytes bear male and female sex organs called antheridia and archegonia.

questions.question: The megasporangium of naked seed plants are called
questions.question_raw: The megasporangium of naked seed plants are called
questions.explanation: The cones bearing megasporophylls with ovules or megasporangia are called macrosporangiate or female strobili.

questions.question: The cone of Gymnosperms represent the:
questions.question_raw: The cone of Gymnosperms represent the:
questions.explanation: In gymnosperms, male and female cones are formed that produce male and female gametes. The strobilus bearing microsporophylls and microsporangia are called microsporangia or male strobilus. The cones bearing megasporophylls with ovules or megasporangia are called microsporangia or female strobilus. So, the cone of gymnosperms represents the reproductive organs.

questions.question: The main plant body of the bryophyte is _______.
questions.question_raw: The main plant body of the bryophyte is _______.
