questions.explanation: The main plant body of the bryophyte is haploid. It produces gametes, hence is called a gametophyte.

questions.question: Which plant has a fungal association in the form of mycorrhiza?
questions.question_raw: Which plant has a fungal association in the form of mycorrhiza?
questions.explanation: Roots in some genera of gymnosperms have a fungal association in the form of mycorrhizal (Pinus).

questions.question: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.explanation: The sporophytes bear sporangia that are subtended by leaf-like appendages called sporophylls.

questions.question: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called ________.
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of having two types of spores by the same plant is called heterospory. Male spores are small (micro) and female spores are comparatively larger (macro) than males like Selaginella and Salvinia are heterosporous.

questions.question: In mosses, the sex organs arise from:
questions.question_raw: In mosses, the sex organs arise from:
questions.explanation: In sexual reproduction, the sex organs antheridia and archegonia are produced at the apex of the leafy shoots.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

questions.question: Liverworts are closely related to______.
questions.question_raw: Liverworts are closely related to______.
questions.explanation: The bryophytes are divided into liverworts and mosses so they are closely related to each other.

questions.question: Apophysis of moss is:
questions.question_raw: Apophysis of moss is:
questions.explanation:
The sporophyte of moss is differentiated into three parts - foot, seta, and capsule. The capsule, in turn, is differentiated into 3 regions.
 Apophysis - the sterile basal portion of the capsule, it is in continuity with seta
 Theca or body of capsule, which is the fertile region, and
 Operculum the apical region of capsule.
Apophysis is rich in chloroplast and thus the photosynthetic region of moss, it also provides nutrition to the developing sporangium.

questions.question: On germination, moss spore produce _________.
questions.question_raw: On germination, moss spore produce _________.
questions.explanation: The spore of moss germinates to produce protonema. It is a creeping, green, branched, and frequently filamentous stage, which develops into secondary protonema as a lateral bud. This stage bears sex organs.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a vascular cryptogam?
questions.explanation: A Cryptogam is a plant that reproduces by spores without flowers or seeds. Most of the cryptogamous plants lack vascular bundles i.e xylem and phloem but some member of pteridophytes like Equisetum contain vascular bundle.

questions.question: Which one is living fossil _______.
questions.question_raw: Which one is living fossil _______.
questions.explanation: Ginkgo is a living fossil. This plant belongs to the gymnosperms division. It is considered as one of the oldest plants known and still surviving in some parts of the world.

questions.question: Which plant division is known as amphibians _________.
questions.question_raw: Which plant division is known as amphibians _________.
questions.explanation: Bryophytes are called amphibians of the plant kingdom as they can grow in both land and water. Water is essential for the completion of the life cycle as they release antherozoids into the water where they come in contact with archegonium to produce a zygote.

questions.question: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.question_raw: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called ______.
questions.explanation: The yellow or brown spots which have sporangia in fern are called sori. Spores are produced inside these sporangia to produce haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: In gymnosperms, cross-pollination is accompanied by-
questions.question_raw: In gymnosperms, cross-pollination is accompanied by-
questions.explanation: In Gymnosperms, cross-pollination is mainly accompanied by wind. The pollen grains are produced in large quantities and generally lighter.

questions.question: Greatest extent of adaptation to various environments is found in_______.
questions.question_raw: Greatest extent of adaptation to various environments is found in_______.
questions.explanation: Adaptation is essential for the survival of the living organism. The greatest extent of adaption in various environmental conditions is found in Angiosperms. Angiosperms are found in all kinds of environments and altitudes.

questions.question: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by ________.
questions.question_raw: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by ________.
questions.explanation: The pollen tubes enter the embryo-sac where two male gametes are discharged. One of the male gametes fuses with the egg cell to form a zygote (syngamy). The other male gamete fuses with the diploid secondary nucleus to produce the triploid primary endosperm nucleus (PEN). Because of the involvement of two fusions, this event is termed as double fertilization.

questions.question: In dicots, each embryo-sac consist of:
questions.question_raw: In dicots, each embryo-sac consist of:

options.label: A
options.text: Egg apparatus, polar nuclei and endosperm

options.label: D
options.text: Egg apparatus, antipodal cells and polar nuclei

correct_option_texts: Egg apparatus, antipodal cells and polar nuclei
questions.explanation: In dicots, each embryo-sac consists of egg apparatus (ovum and two synergids) located at micropylar end, three antipodal cells located at the chalazal end and two polar nuclei at the centre.

questions.question: A plant that forms seeds but no fruits are ______.
questions.question_raw: A plant that forms seeds but no fruits are ______.
questions.explanation: Pinus belongs to division gymnosperms. In gymnosperms, seeds are formed but not inside the fruit. They are also called as plants with naked seeds.

questions.question: A plant that forms fruits but not seeds are ______.
questions.question_raw: A plant that forms fruits but not seeds are ______.
questions.explanation: Generally, seeds are formed inside the fruits. But in banana viable seeds are not present. It is a parthenocarpic fruit.

questions.question: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.
questions.question_raw: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.

options.label: A
options.text: Absence of rasin duct and leaf venation

options.label: B
options.text: Presence of vessels elements and the absence of archegonia

correct_option_texts: Presence of vessels elements and the absence of archegonia
questions.explanation: Gnetum belongs to order Gnetales. In gymnosperms except Gnetales xylem consists of xylem parenchyma and tracheids with bordered pits but lacks vessels. This shows that Gnetales are the most advanced among gymnosperms. They lack archegonia in female gametophytes thus showing similarity with angiosperms and acts as a connecting link between the gymnosperms and angiosperms.

questions.question: Top shaped multinucleate male gametes and mature seeds with one embryo having two cotyledons are characteristic features of_______.
questions.question_raw: Top shaped multinucleate male gametes and mature seeds with one embryo having two cotyledons are characteristic features of_______.
questions.explanation: Cycas produces top-shaped multinucleate male gametes and mature seeds with one embryo having two cotyledons. Although, male and female gametes are generally uninucleate in gymnosperms.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Plant Kingdom

questions.question: All seed bearing plant, alternation of generation is ________.
questions.question_raw: All seed bearing plant, alternation of generation is ________.
questions.explanation: In diplontic life cycle, diploid sporophyte is the dominant, photosynthetic, independent phase of the plant. The gametophytic phase is represented by the single to few-celled haploid gametophyte .All seed bearing plants i.e., gymnosperms and angiosperms exhibits diplontic life cycle.

questions.question: The sperms of ________ are largest in the biological world.
questions.question_raw: The sperms of ________ are largest in the biological world.
questions.explanation: The sperms of male gamete of Cycas are the largest in the biological world. It is top-shaped. In Cycas revoluta the size of the sperm ranges between 180 to 210 μm and they are clearly visible to the naked eye.

questions.question: Haplontic life cycle is seen in_______.
questions.question_raw: Haplontic life cycle is seen in_______.
questions.explanation: In the haplontic life cycle, the dominant, photosynthetic phase is the free-living gametophyte which is seen in Volvox, Spirogyra, and Chlamydomonas.

questions.question: Thallus of Riccia is ________.
questions.question_raw: Thallus of Riccia is ________.
questions.explanation: A dominant, independent, photosynthetic, thalloid, or erect phase of Bryophytes and pteridophytes is represented by a haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: Pteridophytes differ from mosses in having:
questions.question_raw: Pteridophytes differ from mosses in having:
questions.explanation: In bryophytes, the dominant phase in the life cycle is the gametophytic plant body. However, in pteridophytes, the main plant body is a sporophyte which is differentiated into true root, stem and leaves.

questions.question: All seed-bearing plant, alternation of generation is_________.
questions.question_raw: All seed-bearing plant, alternation of generation is_________.
questions.explanation: All seed-bearing plants i.e., gymnosperms and angiosperms follow diplontic alternation of a generation where the diploid sporophyte is the dominant, photosynthetic, independent phase of the plant. The gametophytic phase is represented by the single to the few-celled haploid gametophyte.

questions.question: Megasporangium of seed plants are called:
questions.question_raw: Megasporangium of seed plants are called:
questions.explanation: Megasporangium of seed plants is called an ovule. The ovule is present inside the ovary which becomes seed on maturation after fertilization. It produces megasporocytes or Megaspore mother cells that yield megaspores.

questions.question: Angiosperm: roots :: Rhodophyta :?
questions.question_raw: Angiosperm: roots :: Rhodophyta :?
questions.explanation: Angiosperms plants have roots to hold the soil and remain erect. Similarly, Rhodophytes have holdfast by which they remain attached with substratum to absorb water and minerals.

questions.question: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by _____________.
questions.question_raw: Primary endosperm nucleus is formed by _____________.
questions.explanation: The fusion of two polar nuclei with one male gamete is called triple fusion. The primary endosperm nucleus is formed by triple fusion and divide mitotically to form endosperm.

questions.question: The phloem of angiosperms differ from that of other vascular plants by the presence of:
questions.question_raw: The phloem of angiosperms differ from that of other vascular plants by the presence of:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms and lower vascular plants have sieve cells as their sieve elements, while sieve tubes and companion cells are characteristic of flowering plants (angiosperms). Phloem transports food from leaves to all other parts of plants.

questions.question: Some plants are dependent on the larger plant for space only. Such plants are called as ________.
questions.question_raw: Some plants are dependent on the larger plant for space only. Such plants are called as ________.
questions.explanation: Epiphytes are dependent on other large plants for space or physical support. It remains to attach with other plants but does not obtain food or another nutrient from it so they are not parasitic on the supporting plants.

questions.question: Which of the following includes a diploid structure?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following includes a diploid structure?
questions.explanation: Gametophytes and capsule are diploid structure and alternate with a haploid structure called spores by reduction division.

questions.question: Non-flowering plants belong to:
questions.question_raw: Non-flowering plants belong to:
questions.explanation: The cryptogams are flowerless and seedless plants. They are simple plants like algae, mosses, and ferns which do not produce flowers, fruits, and seeds. Cryptogams are considered as lower plants.

questions.question: In which one of the following, male and female gametophytes do not have free living independent existence?
questions.question_raw: In which one of the following, male and female gametophytes do not have free living independent existence?
questions.explanation: In Cedrus plants, male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence. They remain attached to sporophytic forms to obtain nutrients.

questions.question: Fusion between one large and non-motile female gamete with smaller, motile male gamete is termed as______.
questions.question_raw: Fusion between one large and non-motile female gamete with smaller, motile male gamete is termed as______.
questions.explanation: Oogamy is a form of anisogamy (heterogamy) in which the female gamete (e.g. egg cell) is significantly larger than the male gamete and is non-motile.

chapters.name: Animal Kingdom
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

questions.question: Which phylum contains coelomic and triploblastic animals?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum contains coelomic and triploblastic animals?
questions.explanation: Those animals in which the developing embryo has a three germinal layer, mesoderm in between the ectoderm and endoderm, are called triploblastic animals, and the absence of an internal body cavity is called coelomic.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out based on coelom: Annelids, Molluscs, Platyhelminthes, Arthropods.
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one out based on coelom: Annelids, Molluscs, Platyhelminthes, Arthropods.
questions.explanation: Animals possessing coelom are called coelomates such as annelids, molluscs, arthropods, echinoderms, hemichordates and chordates. In Platyhelmites coelom is absent.

questions.question: In Coelenterates, the cell shows:
questions.question_raw: In Coelenterates, the cell shows:
questions.explanation: In coelenterates, the arrangement of cells is more complex. Here, the cells performing the same function are arranged into tissues, hence is called tissue level of organization.

questions.question: The cross-section of the body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:-
questions.question_raw: The cross-section of the body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:-
questions.explanation: In some animals, the body cavity is not lined by mesoderm, instead, the mesoderm is present as scattered pouches in between the ectoderm and endoderm. Such a body cavity is called pseudocoelom and the animals possessing them are called pseudocoelomates, e.g.,aschelminthes such as round worm.

questions.question: The largest phylum of kingdom animalia is ________.
questions.question_raw: The largest phylum of kingdom animalia is ________.
questions.explanation: Arthropoda is the largest phylum of kingdom Animalia. It includes all insects, arachnids, myriapods, and crustaceans. They are characterized by jointed limbs and cuticles.

questions.question: Egg of reptiles and birds are:
questions.question_raw: Egg of reptiles and birds are:
questions.explanation: The eggs of reptiles and birds are polyecithal because they contain a large amount of yolk inside the egg.

questions.question: Animals of class Mammalia have:
questions.question_raw: Animals of class Mammalia have:
questions.explanation: The animals of class Mammalia have seven cervical vertebrae and a four-chambered heart. Mammalian is characterized by the presence of mammary glands to feed their young ones.

questions.question: The alimentary canal is absent in:
questions.question_raw: The alimentary canal is absent in:
questions.explanation: The alimentary canal is absent in Taenia Solium. Taenia Solium is an obligate parasite and obtains digested food from the small intestine of the human being as well as pigs. Pick the animals with true placenta.

questions.question: Pick the animals with true placenta__________.
questions.question_raw: Pick the animals with true placenta__________.
questions.explanation: The true placenta is present in Mongoose. Placenta help in the transfer of nutrients and respiratory gases from the mother to the growing foetus.

questions.question: The air bladder is present in_______.
questions.question_raw: The air bladder is present in_______.
questions.explanation: The air bladder is present in Actinopterygii. It is also known as ray-finned fish due to the presence of fin rays.

questions.question: Book lungs are respiratory organ of______.
questions.question_raw: Book lungs are respiratory organ of______.
questions.explanation: Book lung is a type of respiratory organ used for atmospheric gas exchange that is found in Arachnida such as scorpions and spiders.

questions.question: Which of the following have notochord throughout life?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following have notochord throughout life?
questions.explanation: Amphioxus or lancelets are fish-like marine chordates found in shallow temperate and tropical seas. They have notochord throughout their life.

questions.question: The aquatic organisms that can actively swim at will against the water current are:
questions.question_raw: The aquatic organisms that can actively swim at will against the water current are:
questions.explanation: Necton is an aquatic organism that can actively swim at will against the water current. Benthos and planktons float with the water current and not against the water current.

questions.question: Haemocoel is found in:
questions.question_raw: Haemocoel is found in:
questions.explanation: Haemocoel is the main body cavity of invertebrates such as insects. It is also found in Pila which do not contain the blood circulating system.

questions.question: An egg-laying animal grouped under mammals is:
questions.question_raw: An egg-laying animal grouped under mammals is:
questions.explanation: Most of the animals belonging to class Mammalia are viviparous, produces young ones. But, Duck-billed platypus belongs to mammalian class but lay eggs.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

questions.question: When the coelomic fluid serves as a skeleton it is known as__________.
questions.question_raw: When the coelomic fluid serves as a skeleton it is known as__________.
questions.explanation: Coelomic fluids serve as a skeleton in some insects called as a hydrostatic skeleton that helps in maintaining shape and size of the body of organisms in the absence of bone or cartilage.

questions.question: Which one of the following group of animals is correctly matched with its characteristics without any exception?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following group of animals is correctly matched with its characteristics without any exception?

options.label: A
options.text: Mammalia give birth to young ones

options.label: B
options.text: Reptilia possess a 3-chambered heart with incompletely divided ventricle

options.label: C
options.text: Chordates possess a mouth with an upper and lower jaw

questions.explanation: All cartilaginous fish or Chondrichthyes possess a cartilaginous endoskeleton. But some reptiles have a four-chambered heart. Chordates may be jawless and some mammals lay eggs also.

questions.question: Animals which possess cleidoic egg exhibit:
questions.question_raw: Animals which possess cleidoic egg exhibit:
questions.explanation: The cleidoic egg is the type of egg that reptiles and birds lay. These eggs exhibit internal fertilization and external development.

questions.question: Which of these is a freshwater sponge?
questions.question_raw: Which of these is a freshwater sponge?
questions.explanation: Spongilla is a genus of freshwater sponges in the family Spongillidae found in lakes and slow streams. Sponges of the genus Spongilla attach themselves to rocks and logs and filter the water for various small aquatic organisms such as protozoans, bacteria, and other free-floating pond life. Unlike marine sponges, freshwater sponges are exposed to far more adverse and variable environmental conditions, so they have developed gemmules as a means of dormancy.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a living fossil?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a living fossil?
questions.explanation: Limulus (the king crab or horse-shoe crab) continues to remain unchanged for the past 190 million years. It is, therefore, also called living fossil. Excretion occurs through four pairs of coxal glands. Respiration occurs through book gills.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest phylum of kingdom Animalia?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is the largest phylum of kingdom Animalia?
questions.explanation: Arthropoda is the largest phylum of Animalia which includes insects.

questions.question: Petromyzon is included under_______.
questions.question_raw: Petromyzon is included under_______.
questions.explanation: Petromyzon is a jawless vertebrate and hence included under Agnatha.

questions.question: Which phylum has a water vascular system as a characteristic feature?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has a water vascular system as a characteristic feature?
questions.explanation: The most distinctive feature of Echinodermata is the presence of the water vascular system.

questions.question: What is common among Liver fluke, Planarians, and Tapeworms?
questions.question_raw: What is common among Liver fluke, Planarians, and Tapeworms?

options.label: B
options.text: All of them are found in gut

options.label: C
options.text: All of them have flattened bodies

options.label: D
options.text: All of them have a coelom

correct_option_texts: All of them have flattened bodies
questions.explanation: All of them belong to Phylum Platyhelminthes and hence have dorsoventrally flattened bodies and so, are called flatworms.

questions.question: Locomotory organ of Annelida is:
questions.question_raw: Locomotory organ of Annelida is:
questions.explanation: Locomotory organ of annelia is parapodia. Parapodium are in form of lateral fleshy protrusions on the body surface that help in movement and capturing food.

questions.question: Which among the following is called Portuguese man-of-war?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is called Portuguese man-of-war?
questions.explanation: The Portuguese man-of-war is a siphonophore, an animal that is made up of a colony of organisms working together. Physalia is called Portuguese man-of-war. It is also called a blue bottle or floating terror it is found floating on the water surface. "The name comes from the uppermost polyp, a gas-filled bladder, or pneumatophore, which sits above the water and somewhat looks like an old warship at full sail. They are also, known as bluebottles for the purple-blue colour of their pneumatophores" (A Portuguese man of war). The tentacles can extend to 165 feet, although, the average length is 30 feet (Portuguese Man-of-War Physalia physalis).

questions.question: Which phylum has bioluminescence as its well-marked feature?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has bioluminescence as its well-marked feature?
questions.explanation: The property of a living organism to emit light is known as bioluminescence and is well-marked in ctenophores.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out: Roundworm, Hookworm, Filaria worm, Earthworm.
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one out: Roundworm, Hookworm, Filaria worm, Earthworm.
questions.explanation: Earthworm belongs to phylum Annelida whereas all other worms belong to phylum Aschelminthes.

questions.question: A marine cartilaginous fish that can produce electric current is:
questions.question_raw: A marine cartilaginous fish that can produce electric current is:
questions.explanation: Torpedo is a genus of rays, commonly known as electric rays, torpedo rays, or torpedoes. They are slow-moving bottom-dwellers capable of generating electricity as a defence and feeding mechanism.

questions.question: Which phylum has balance organs?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has balance organs?
questions.explanation: Sensory organs like antennae, eyes (compound and simple), statocysts, or balance organs are present in phylum Arthropoda.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

questions.question: The limbless amphibian is:
questions.question_raw: The limbless amphibian is:
questions.explanation: Ichthyophis is an amphibian which is limbless. Sometimes called as Asian caecilians. They are found in southeast Asia and the southern Philippines.

questions.question: The air bladder is absent in:
questions.question_raw: The air bladder is absent in:
questions.explanation: The air bladder is absent in Chondrichthyes that help in floating in the water.

questions.question: The phylum in which adults exhibit radial symmetry and larva exhibit bilateral symmetry is:
questions.question_raw: The phylum in which adults exhibit radial symmetry and larva exhibit bilateral symmetry is:
questions.explanation:
The animals of Phylum - Echinodermata have an endoskeleton of calcareous ossicles and, hence, the name Echinodermata. All are marine with the organ-system level of organization. The adult echinoderms are radially symmetrical but larvae are bilaterally symmetrical.
They are triploblastic and coelomate animals. The digestive system is complete with a mouth on the lower (ventral) side and anus on the upper (dorsal) side.

questions.question: Which group of chordates possesses sucking and circular mouth without jaws?
questions.question_raw: Which group of chordates possesses sucking and circular mouth without jaws?
questions.explanation: The organism beloging to class Cyclostomes have a sucking and circular mouth without jaws.They are primitive vertebrates. The cyclostomes are marine or fresh-water vertebrates.

questions.question: Which among the following is oviparous?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is oviparous?
questions.explanation: Platypus is the oviparous that lays eggs.

questions.question: Which phylum has comb plates for locomotion as their distinctive feature?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum has comb plates for locomotion as their distinctive feature?
questions.explanation: The body of phylum Ctenophora bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates, which help in locomotion.

questions.question: A fish that has an electric organ is:
questions.question_raw: A fish that has an electric organ is:
questions.explanation: Torpedo fish has electric organ. They are known for being capable of producing an electric discharge ranging from 8 to 220 volts depending on species.

questions.question: In which of the organism, internal fertilization is seen?
questions.question_raw: In which of the organism, internal fertilization is seen?
questions.explanation: Most of the animals are not able to fertilize their own egg by sperms but in liver fluke, self-fertilization takes place. Earthworm also possesses both male and female reproductive organs in the same individual but not able to fertilize its own egg.

questions.question: The endoskeleton is fully ossified and the long bones are pneumatic in:
questions.question_raw: The endoskeleton is fully ossified and the long bones are pneumatic in:
questions.explanation: The endoskeleton is fully ossified (bony) and the long bones are hollow with air cavities (pneumatic) in class aves.

questions.question: The presence of milk-producing glands is a unique characteristic feature of the class:
questions.question_raw: The presence of milk-producing glands is a unique characteristic feature of the class:
questions.explanation: Mammalia is found in a variety of habitats - polar ice caps, deserts, mountains, forests, grasslands, and dark caves. Some of them have adapted to fly or live in water. The most unique mammalian characteristic is the presence of milk-producing glands (mammary glands) by which the young ones are nourished. They have two pairs of limbs, adapted for walking, running, climbing, burrowing, swimming, or flying.

questions.question: Which among the following is monoecious?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is monoecious?
questions.explanation: Earthworms and leeches are monoecious, contain both male and female reproductive organs in the same individual.

questions.question: Which phylum shows this type of germinal layers shown below?
questions.question_raw: Which phylum shows this type of germinal layers shown below?
questions.explanation: The germinal layer shows diploblastic. Phylum Coelenterata shows this type of germinal layers.

questions.question: Which phyla does not possess a coelom?
questions.question_raw: Which phyla does not possess a coelom?
questions.explanation: Phylum Platyhelminthes does not possess a coelom. The coelom is the body cavity in between organs. Animals with coelom are called coelomate and those without it are called acoelomate.

questions.question: Bat and whales have ________ chambered heart.
questions.question_raw: Bat and whales have ________ chambered heart.
questions.explanation: Bat and whales have a four-chambered heart. Upper two chambers are called the atrium and lower two chambers are called the ventricle. All mammals have a four-chambered heart.

questions.question: Which one of the following does not belonging to Urochordata?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following does not belonging to Urochordata?
questions.explanation: Lancelets are classified in the phylum Chordata, subphylum Cephalochordata. lancelet, the name for small, fishlike lower chordate also called amphioxus it shows many affinities with the vertebrates. There are about 30 lancelet species, most belonging to the genus Branchiostoma (formerly Amphioxus). Lancelets are usually about 1 in. (2.5 cm) long, with transparent bodies tapered at both ends. There is no distinct head and no paired fins.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

questions.question: What is the main excretory organ in insects?
questions.question_raw: What is the main excretory organ in insects?
questions.explanation: The insects belong to phylum Arthropoda and hence, the excretory organ is Malphighian tubules.

questions.question: Which among the following is a gregarious pest?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is a gregarious pest?
questions.explanation: Locust (Locusta) is a gregarious pest belonging to phylum Arthropoda.

questions.question: Identify the animal given below. Mention its phylum.
questions.question_raw: Identify the animal given below. Mention its phylum.
questions.explanation: The animal is identified as Euspongia which belong to phylum Porifera.

questions.question: The excretory organ of Saccoglossus is:
questions.question_raw: The excretory organ of Saccoglossus is:
questions.explanation: Saccoglossus belong to phylum Hemichordata and hence the excretory organ is proboscis gland.

questions.question: A reptile has a four-chambered heart is:
questions.question_raw: A reptile has a four-chambered heart is:
questions.explanation: Reptiles generally possess three-chamber heart, two upper chamber and single lower chamber. But in crocodile, the heart is four-chambered having two atrium and two ventricles.

questions.question: In Molluscs, the rasping feeding organ is:
questions.question_raw: In Molluscs, the rasping feeding organ is:
questions.explanation: The mouth of molluscs contains a file-like rasping organ for feeding, called a radula.

questions.question: Bony fishes have sac-like outgrowth, called air bladder arising from the dorsal wall of the oesophagus. It is used to:
questions.question_raw: Bony fishes have sac-like outgrowth, called air bladder arising from the dorsal wall of the oesophagus. It is used to:

options.label: B
options.text: Maintain buoyancy for swimming Swim constantly to avoid sinking

options.label: D
options.text: Maintain balance and to swim up and down Maintain buoyancy for swimming

correct_option_texts: Maintain balance and to swim up and down Maintain buoyancy for swimming
questions.explanation: Air bladder is present in body fish that help them in maintaining balance and to swim up and down. Air bladder arises from the dorsal wall of the oesophagus.

questions.question: Which is the second largest phylum?
questions.question_raw: Which is the second largest phylum?
questions.explanation: Phylum Mollusca is the second largest phylum after Arthropoda.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a poisonous snake?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a poisonous snake?
questions.explanation: Examples of poisonous snakes are Naja(Cobra), Bangarus(Krait), Vipera(Viper). Testudo is a non-poisonous snake. It is found in Europe.

questions.question: Which of the following does not have an excretory system?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following does not have an excretory system?
questions.explanation: Asterias does not have an excretory system. Asterias includes starfish or sea star. They are exclusively marine.

questions.question: Identify the animal in the figure given below and the phylum to which the animal belongs to?
questions.question_raw: Identify the animal in the figure given below and the phylum to which the animal belongs to?
questions.explanation: The animal is dentified as octopus which belongs to phylum Mollusca.

questions.question: Choose the odd one out based on the levels of the organization: Annelids, Arthropods, Platyhelminthes, Molluscs.
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd one out based on the levels of the organization: Annelids, Arthropods, Platyhelminthes, Molluscs.
questions.explanation: The organ level is exhibited by members of Platyhelminthes and other higher phyla where tissues are grouped together to form organs, each specialized for a particular function. In animals like Annelids, Arthropods, Molluscs, organs have associated to form functional systems, each system concerned with a specific physiological function. This pattern is called an organ system level of organization.

questions.question: Ancestors of mammals belongs to:
questions.question_raw: Ancestors of mammals belongs to:
questions.explanation: The ancestor of mammals belongs to class Therapsida. Therapsida is a group of synapsids that includes mammals and their ancestors.

questions.question: Bat is member of the order ________.
questions.question_raw: Bat is member of the order ________.
questions.explanation: Bat is a member of order Chiroptera of class Mammalia. Bats are only mammals to have achieved powered to flight in which forelimb is modified as wings.

questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.explanation: Insects have the highest number of species in nature. Insect belongs to arthropods family of class Mammalia. They are characterized by jointed legs.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Animal Kingdom

questions.question: One animal that does not perform locomotion is______.
questions.question_raw: One animal that does not perform locomotion is______.
questions.explanation: Sycon does not perform locomotion, although it is an animal. Sycon remains attached to the substratum and does not move from one place to another.

questions.question: The larvae of urochordates is called:
questions.question_raw: The larvae of urochordates is called:
questions.explanation: The larvae of urochordates are called tadpole. Tadpole change into adults by the process of metamorphosis. The structure of tadpole is totally different from the adult ones.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called ________.
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: Species going to extinct due to low reproductive rate is ________.
questions.question_raw: Species going to extinct due to low reproductive rate is ________.
questions.explanation: The giant panda is going to extinct due to low reproductive rate. They live in mountain ranges in central China. There reproductively rate is very less due to climatic conditions.

questions.question:
Which one is correct?
 Sea Lily is an echinoderm.
 Blue-green algae are a kind of fungus.
 Maidenhair tree is an angiosperm.
 Sea horse is related to dolphins.
questions.question_raw:
Which one is correct?

  1. Sea Lily is an echinoderm.
  2. Blue-green algae are a kind of fungus.
  3. Maidenhair tree is an angiosperm.
  4. Sea horse is related to dolphins.
questions.explanation: Sea Lily is an echinoderm having spine all over the body. Blue-green algae is a class of bacteria. Maidenhair tree is pteridophytes and the sea horse is not related to the dolphin.

questions.question: Lac is produced by lac insect as:
questions.question_raw: Lac is produced by lac insect as:

options.label: C
options.text: Excess food oozing out of the body

questions.explanation: Lac is obtained by lac insects as secretion from the body. This secretion is wounded around the cocoon of lac insects.

questions.question: The life-span of honey bee drone is:
questions.question_raw: The life-span of honey bee drone is:
questions.explanation: The life-span is the time period from birth to death of an organism. The life-span of honey bee drone is 1-2 months only. Queen has the longest life span in all three kinds of honey bees.

questions.question: Largest mammal is ________.
questions.question_raw: Largest mammal is ________.
questions.explanation: The blue whale is the largest mammals about 30 meter in length and 180 tons in weight.

questions.question: What is the main excretory waste in insects?
questions.question_raw: What is the main excretory waste in insects?
questions.explanation: The main nitrogenous excretory wastes of insects are uric acid. Uric acid is released in concentrated form. Other animals release nitrogenous wastes in form of ammonia and urea.

questions.question: Salamander belongs to the class:
questions.question_raw: Salamander belongs to the class:
questions.explanation: Salamanders are a group of amphibians typically characterized by a lizard-like appearance, with slender bodies, blunt snouts, short limbs projecting at right angles to the body, and the presence of a tail in both larvae and adults.

questions.question: Which two teeth are modified into tusk in elephants?
questions.question_raw: Which two teeth are modified into tusk in elephants?
questions.explanation: In elephants, two upper front teeth are modified into tusk of elephants. The continuous growth of tusk is enabled by formative tissues in the apical opening of the root of the teeth.

questions.question: Which molluscs is without shell?
questions.question_raw: Which molluscs is without shell?
questions.explanation: Octopus belongs to molluscs but does not contain the shell. All other molluscs possess hard calcareous cells around their body to act as a protective covering.

questions.question: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?
questions.question_raw: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?

options.label: A
options.text: Have gill slit at some stage of life

options.label: B
options.text: Lay eggs and guard them till they hatch

correct_option_texts: Have gill slit at some stage of life
questions.explanation: Crocodile and penguins are similar to whale and dogfish in having gill slits at some stage of their life cycle which is used as respiratory organs.

questions.question: The cross-section of body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:
questions.question_raw: The cross-section of body of an invertebrate is given below. Identify the animal which has this body plan:
questions.explanation: The given diagram is cross section of roundworm, an invertebrate that possesses coelom and centrally located alimentary canal.

questions.question: Which of the following is most poisonous?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is most poisonous?
questions.explanation: Heloderma is the most poisonous, which is a verminous lizard native to the southwestern united states and Mexico.

chapters.name: Morphology of Flowering Plants
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: Monstera has:
questions.question_raw: Monstera has:
questions.explanation: In some plants, like grass, Monstera, and the banyan tree, roots arise from parts of the plant other than the radicle and are called adventitious roots.

questions.question: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called:
questions.question_raw: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called:
questions.explanation: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called the root cap.

questions.question: The part that develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed is:
questions.question_raw: The part that develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed is:
questions.explanation: The stem develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed.

questions.question: Rhizophora possess________.
questions.question_raw: Rhizophora possess________.
questions.explanation: Rhizophora possess Pneumatophores

questions.question: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called
questions.question_raw: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called
questions.explanation: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called adventitious roots.

questions.question: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called _______.
questions.explanation: The leaf base bear two lateral small leaf like structures called stipules.

questions.question: Potato is a modified______.
questions.question_raw: Potato is a modified______.
questions.explanation: Potato is a modified stem as it contains nodes in the form of eyes on potato tubers which germinate to produce new potato plants if placed in suitable conditions.

questions.question: The type of aestivation shown by the diagram below is
questions.question_raw: The type of aestivation shown by the diagram below is
questions.explanation:
The diagram shows which is vexillary aestivation.
Vexillary aestivation is a mode of aestivation in which one large upper petal folds over, and covers, the other smaller petals, as in most papilionaceous plants.

questions.question: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.question_raw: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.explanation: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight, and pointed thorns in Citrus and Bougainvillea. They protect plants from browsing animals.

questions.question: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in _____.
questions.question_raw: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in _____.
questions.explanation: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in aquatic plants such as Pistia and Eicchornia.

questions.question: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.question_raw: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.explanation: The petiole help hold the blade to light. Long thin flexible petioles allow leaf blades to flutter in wind, thereby cooling the leaf and bringing fresh air to the leaf surface.

questions.question: Which part of the plant store food in garlic?
questions.question_raw: Which part of the plant store food in garlic?
questions.explanation: The fleshy leaves of onion and garlic store food.

questions.question:
The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is
 Root hair zone
 Zone of meristem
 Root cap zone
 Zone of maturation
 Zone of elongation
questions.question_raw:
The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is

  1. Root hair zone
  2. Zone of meristem
  3. Root cap zone
  4. Zone of maturation
  5. Zone of elongation
questions.explanation: The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is root cap zone, region of meristematic activity, region of elongation, region of maturation and root hair region.

questions.question: Phylloclade are:
questions.question_raw: Phylloclade are:

options.label: A
options.text: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth

correct_option_texts: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth
questions.explanation: Phylloclade are green succulent stems of indefinite growth that perform the function of photosynthesis due to the presence of chlorophyll.

questions.question: Select the insectivorous plant from the following:
questions.question_raw: Select the insectivorous plant from the following:
questions.explanation: Those plants that eat insect to fulfil its protein requirement are called insectivorous plants. They have modified leaves to capture insects. Drosera is an insectivorous plant.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: 120^o phyllotaxy occurs in _________.
questions.question_raw: 120o phyllotaxy occurs in _________.
questions.explanation: Phyllotaxy is the arrangement of leaves on a plant stem. 120^o phyllotaxy occurs in a Tristichous arrangement in which three leaves are arranged at each node.

questions.question: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.question_raw: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.explanation: Parallel venation is the characteristic features of monocots. Dicots possess reticulate venation. The arrangement of veins on the leaf is called venation.

questions.question: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.explanation: Leaves perform the function of photosynthesis, exchange of gases, and transpiration to lose excess of water. The formation of new buds is not a function of leaves.

questions.question: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.question_raw: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.explanation: The stem of Ocimum sanctum or tulsi is quadrangular in shape. The stem is covered with hairy projection.

questions.question: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.question_raw: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.explanation: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitcher of Nepenthes are a modification of leaves. They are modified to catch insects that are insectivorous plants.

questions.question: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.question_raw: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.explanation: Dischidia

questions.question: Inflorescence which shows indefinite growth and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Inflorescence which shows indefinite growth and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point is called ________.
questions.explanation: In racemose inflorescence indefinite growth occurs and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point.

questions.question: A scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit is called _________.
questions.question_raw: A scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit is called _________.
questions.explanation: The hilum is a scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit.

questions.question: Name the type of thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals and sepals are born below.
questions.question_raw: Name the type of thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals and sepals are born below.
questions.explanation: In hypergamous flowers, the ovary is at the tops and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below on the thalamus. The ovary is called superior.

questions.question: The fruit develops to form a whole inflorescence is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fruit develops to form a whole inflorescence is called ________.
questions.explanation: True fruit is the fruit that develops from ovary only. In composite fruits, the whole inflorescence develops into fruits as in case of jackfruit.

questions.question: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.question_raw: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.explanation: In angiosperms, a hypanthium is a structure where basal portions of the calyx, the corolla, and the stamens form a cup-shaped tube.

questions.question: Ancestors of mammals belong to________.
questions.question_raw: Ancestors of mammals belong to________.
questions.explanation: The ancestor of mammals belongs to class Therapsida. Therapsida is a group of synapsids that includes mammals and their ancestors.

questions.question: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.question_raw: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.explanation: Bat is a member of the order Chiroptera of class Mammalia. Bats are only mammals to have achieved powered flight in which forelimb is modified as wings.

questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.explanation: Insects have the highest number of species in nature. Insect belongs to arthropods family of class Mammalia. They are characterized by jointed legs.

questions.question: One animal that does not perform locomotion is ______.
questions.question_raw: One animal that does not perform locomotion is ______.
questions.explanation: Sycon does not perform locomotion, although it is an animal. Sycon remains attached to the substratum and does not move from one place to another.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.question_raw: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.explanation: The locomotory organ of Annelida is parapodia. Parapodium is in the form of lateral fleshy protrusions on the body surface that help in movement and capturing food.

questions.question: The larvae of urochordates are called:
questions.question_raw: The larvae of urochordates are called:
questions.explanation: The larvae of urochordates are called tadpole. Tadpole change into adults by the process of metamorphosis. The structure of tadpole is totally different from the adult ones.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: The limbless amphibian is______.
questions.question_raw: The limbless amphibian is______.
questions.explanation: Ichthyophis is an amphibian which is limbless. Sometimes called as Asian caecilians. They are found in southeast Asia and the southern Philippines.

questions.question: In an inflorescence where flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession, the position of the youngest floral bud in the floral axis shall be
questions.question_raw: In an inflorescence where flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession, the position of the youngest floral bud in the floral axis shall be
questions.explanation: In the acropetal succession of an inflorescence, the youngest floral buds are found at the distal end and the oldest flowers are found at the proximal end.

questions.question: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.question_raw: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.explanation: In basal placentation, the placenta develops at the base of the ovary and a single ovule is attached to it.

questions.question: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.question_raw: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.explanation: Aleurone layer of maize grain is rich in proteins. It is the outermost layer of the endosperm followed by the inner starchy endosperm.

questions.question: Individual component of the perianth are called:
questions.question_raw: Individual component of the perianth are called:
questions.explanation: The flowers in which sepals and petals are not differentiated are called perianth. The individual component of the perianth is called Tepals.

questions.question: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperm is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperm is called ________.
questions.explanation: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperms is called flower. Flower produce seeds and fruits for the propagation of species.

questions.question: The wall of fruit is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The wall of fruit is called ________.
questions.explanation: Fruits are the mature ovary or ovaries of one or more flowers. In fleshy fruits, the outer layer (which is often edible) is the pericarp, which is the tissue that develops from the ovary wall of the flower and surrounds the seeds.

questions.question: The mature seeds of plants such as gram and peas, possess no endosperm, because
questions.question_raw: The mature seeds of plants such as gram and peas, possess no endosperm, because

options.label: A
options.text: Endosperm gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.

options.label: C
options.text: Endosperm is not formed in them.

options.label: D
options.text: Double fertilization does not take place in the plants.

correct_option_texts: Endosperm gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.
questions.explanation: The mature seeds of plants do not possess endosperm because, it gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.

questions.question: The single cotyledon of cereal grain is_______.
questions.question_raw: The single cotyledon of cereal grain is_______.
questions.explanation: In monocotyledonous seeds, the single cotyledon is called as scutellum. It is found in cereal grains like rice, wheat, maize and other monocots.

questions.question: Identify the correct one with respect to the symmetry of the flower.
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct one with respect to the symmetry of the flower.
questions.explanation: When a flower is divided into two equal radial halves in any radial plane passing through the centre, it is said to be actinomorphic e.g., mustard, datura, chilli.

questions.question: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.explanation: When more than one carpel is present, they may be free, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: Parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, where ovules are borne is called:
questions.question_raw: Parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, where ovules are borne is called:
questions.explanation: The placenta is the parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, ovules are borne. Ovules are attached with an ovary at the placenta to get nourishment.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.question_raw: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part. The ovary is one-chambered but it becomes two-chambered due to the formation of the false septum, e.g., mustard and Argemone.

questions.question: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.explanation: In the hypogynous flower, the gynoecium occupies the highest position while the other parts are situated below it. The ovary in such flowers is said to be superior.

questions.question: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.question_raw: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.explanation: When sepals or petals in a whorl just touch one another at the margin, without overlapping, as in Calotropis, it is said to be valvate.

questions.question: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.question_raw: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.explanation: The stamens in a flower may either remain free (polyandrous) or maybe united in varying degrees.

questions.question: Mango is an example of_______.
questions.question_raw: Mango is an example of_______.
questions.explanation: Drupe is an indehiscent fruit in which an outer fleshy part and surrounds a shell of hardened endocarp with a seed. Mango is an example of a drupe.

questions.question: Edible part of Coconut is:
questions.question_raw: Edible part of Coconut is:
questions.explanation: The edible part of Coconut is endosperm and embryo. The water inside the coconut is a cellular embryo and white kernel surrounding it is the main edible part that is the endosperm.

questions.question: A seed is made up of
questions.question_raw: A seed is made up of

options.label: D
options.text: A seed coat and an embryo

correct_option_texts: A seed coat and an embryo
questions.explanation: A seed is made up of a seed coat and an embryo.

questions.question: If the margin of thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of flower arise above the ovary, the flower is said to be:
questions.question_raw: If the margin of thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of flower arise above the ovary, the flower is said to be:
questions.explanation: In epigynous flowers, the margin of the thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of the flower arise above the ovary.

questions.question: The diagram below shows
questions.question_raw: The diagram below shows
questions.explanation: If gynoecium is situated in the centre and otherparts of the flower are located on the rim of the thalamus almost at thesame level, it is called perigynous. The ovary here is said to be half inferior.

questions.question: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.question_raw: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part.

questions.question: The aestivation seen in Solanaceae is_____.
questions.question_raw: The aestivation seen in Solanaceae is_____.
questions.explanation: Valvate aestivation is seen in the family Solanaceae.

questions.question: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by bicarpellarysyncarpous inferior ovary is in the diagram given below, the parts A and B are respectively,
questions.question_raw: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by bicarpellarysyncarpous inferior ovary is in the diagram given below, the parts A and B are respectively,
questions.explanation: The part is identified as Hilum and Micropyle.

questions.question: Which one is a monocarpic in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a monocarpic in nature?
questions.explanation: Monocarpic plants are those that flower, set seeds, and then die. Bamboo is monocarpic in nature that flowers once in a lifetime.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a true nut?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a true nut?
questions.explanation:
A true nut is a fruit composed of a hard shell and an edible seed. The shell does not open to release the seed (indehiscent), e.g., hazelnut, chestnuts.
Walnuts, almonds and pecans are drupes. The fruit of the coconut is a large, dry drupe. Cashew nuts are not true nuts. In the cashew nut, the ovary develops outside of the fruit (false fruit). The groundnut or peanut is not a true nut, but rather a legume. In groundnut, edible the cotyledons remain below ground and act as a storage organ.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: Largest bud is found in______.
questions.question_raw: Largest bud is found in______.
questions.explanation: Cabbage is the bud of the cabbage plant. Cabbage bud is considered as largest bud present in angiosperms plants. A successive layer of leaves covers the buds to form the round structure on condensed stem.

questions.question: The white, translucent, fleshy, and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is _____.
questions.question_raw: The white, translucent, fleshy, and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is _____.
questions.explanation: The white translucent, fleshy and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is called an aril. It is found in a mace of the nutmeg seed.

questions.question: Study of seed is called as______.
questions.question_raw: Study of seed is called as______.
questions.explanation: The study of seed is called Spermology. Study of seed includes shape, size, colour and viability.

questions.question: The edible part of the apple is:
questions.question_raw: The edible part of the apple is:
questions.explanation: The edible part of the apple is fleshy thalamus. Apple is an example of false fruit as it contains thalamus along with ovary.

questions.question: A chambered fruit formed from an inferior ovary and having succulent testa is_______.
questions.question_raw: A chambered fruit formed from an inferior ovary and having succulent testa is_______.
questions.explanation: Pomegranate is the fruit which is formed from inferior ovary having succulent testa. Chambered compartment bears many seeds generally red in colour when ripened.

questions.question: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.question_raw: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.explanation: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.question_raw: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.explanation: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: Sweet potato and potato are an example of:
questions.question_raw: Sweet potato and potato are an example of:
questions.explanation: Sweet potato and potato are an example of analogous organs as they perform the same function that is storing food. But potato is a modified stem but sweet potato is modified root.

questions.question: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.question_raw: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.explanation: Fabaceae are the sources of pulses such as gram, arhar, sem, moong, soybean.

questions.question: The floral formula P_3+3 A_3+3 G_[3] belongs to
questions.question_raw: The floral formula   P3+3 A3+3 G[3]  belongs to
questions.explanation: In Liliaceae, the flower is bisexual and actinomorphic. There are 6 (3+3) tepals and 6 (3+3) stamens with a superior ovary and trilocular with many ovules.

questions.question: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.question_raw: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.explanation: Indigofera is a dye belonging to family Fabaceae.

questions.question: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.question_raw: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.explanation: The members of family Solanaceae bears flower which is pentamerous, actinomorphic, bicarpellary, ovary with oblique septum and berry, or capsule fruit. It includes potato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: Hypanthodium is found in the genus is ________.
questions.question_raw: Hypanthodium is found in the genus is ________.
questions.explanation: Hypanthodium is a special type of inflorescence in which receptacle is fleshy and forms a hollow ball-like structure with an apical opening. This kind of inflorescence is found in genus ficus.

questions.question: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.explanation: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called polygamous flower. The same plant bears all three kinds of flowers.

questions.question: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.explanation: The symmetry in which a flower can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called zygomorphic flower as in pea.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: What is samara?
questions.question_raw: What is samara?

options.label: A
options.text: Fruits having wings formed from other structure

correct_option_texts: Fruits having wings formed from other structure
questions.explanation: Samara is a winged achene, a type of fruit which a flattened wing of fibrous, papery tissue develops from the ovary wall.

questions.question: Unipinnate compound leaves are present in:
questions.question_raw: Unipinnate compound leaves are present in:
questions.explanation: A pinnately compound leaves are one which the midrib bears laterally a number of leaflets. Unipinnate compound leaves are present in neem plant.

questions.question: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called ________.
questions.explanation: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called parietal placentation.

questions.question: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by the bi-carpellary syncarpous inferior ovary is______.
questions.question_raw: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by the bi-carpellary syncarpous inferior ovary is______.
questions.explanation: Cypsela is a single-seeded dry indehiscent fruit that develops one form part of the inferior ovary which is bicarpellary and syncarpous.

questions.question: The science that deals with the study of form, size, colour, structure, and relative position of various parts of an organism is called _________.
questions.question_raw: The science that deals with the study of form, size, colour, structure, and relative position of various parts of an organism is called _________.
questions.explanation: Morphology is the branch of science in which we study about form, size, colour, structure and relative position of various parts of organisms.

questions.question: Name the smallest angiosperm.
questions.question_raw: Name the smallest angiosperm.
questions.explanation: The smallest angiosperm plant is Wolffia. It is an aquatic plant, free-floating, green or yellow-green and without roots.

questions.question: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called ________.
questions.explanation: Stipules are the small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protect the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage. It is a green leafy structure.

questions.question: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.explanation: Zostera is a marine angiosperms plant. The plants have broad leaves that help them in floating on the water surface, the root is poorly developed.

questions.question: Among pea tendril, lemon thorn, and Opuntia spines, which one are homologous structures?
questions.question_raw: Among pea tendril, lemon thorn, and Opuntia spines, which one are homologous structures?
questions.explanation: Homologous structures are those structures in which they have common origin and structure but perform different functions. Pea tendrils and Opuntia spines are homologous structures.

questions.question: The main plant body of banana is:
questions.question_raw: The main plant body of banana is:
questions.explanation: The main plant body of banana is an aggregation of leaf base. The stem is highly reduced and rudimentary present at the base of the plant body.

questions.question: Phylloclade are:
questions.question_raw: Phylloclade are:

options.label: A
options.text: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth

correct_option_texts: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth
questions.explanation: Phylloclade are green succulent stems of indefinite growth that perform the function of photosynthesis due to the presence of chlorophyll.

questions.question: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.explanation: Leaves perform the function of photosynthesis, exchange of gases, and transpiration to lose excess of water. The formation of new buds is not a function of leaves.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.question_raw: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.explanation: When sepals or petals in a whorl just touch one another at the margin, without overlapping, as in Calotropis, it is said to be valvate.

questions.question: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.explanation: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called polygamous flower. The same plant bears all three kinds of flowers.

chapters.name: Anatomy of Flowering Plants
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Anatomy of Flowering Plants

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Anatomy of Flowering Plants

questions.question: The tissue present in all organs of plant is________.
questions.question_raw: The tissue present in all organs of plant is________.
questions.explanation: Generally, all the organs of a plant are made up of parenchyma cells.

questions.question: Which meristem contributes to the formation of the primary plant body?
questions.question_raw: Which meristem contributes to the formation of the primary plant body?
questions.explanation: Both apical meristems and intercalary meristems are primary meristems because they appear early in the life of a plant and contribute to the formation of the primary plant body.

questions.question: Sclereids belongs to_______.
questions.question_raw: Sclereids belongs to_______.
questions.explanation: Sclereids belongs to sclerenchyma complex permanent tissue present in hard parts of plants. They are generally hard and founds in rough fruits and stem.

questions.question: The parenchymatous cells which lie between the xylem and the phloem are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The parenchymatous cells which lie between the xylem and the phloem are called ________.
questions.explanation: Conjunctive tissue is a narrow strip of tissue (parenchyma or sclerenchyma) that lies between xylem and phloem bundles of the root.

questions.question: A specialized parenchymatous cell, which is closely associated with sieve tube elements are:
questions.question_raw: A specialized parenchymatous cell, which is closely associated with sieve tube elements are:
questions.explanation: The companion cells are specialised parenchymatous cells, which are closely associated with sieve tube elements. The sieve tube elements and companion cells are connected by pit fields present between their common longitudinal walls.

questions.question: The meristems which occur at the tips of roots and shoots and produce primary tissues are called _________.
questions.question_raw: The meristems which occur at the tips of roots and shoots and produce primary tissues are called _________.
questions.explanation: Apical meristem is found at the apices, or tips of the plant, both the tip of the shoot and the root, and is a region of actively dividing cells. The definition is easy to remember when you break it down. An apex is a tip, the very end, of something.

questions.question: A group of cells having a common origin and usually performing a common function is:
questions.question_raw: A group of cells having a common origin and usually performing a common function is:
questions.explanation: A tissue is an ensemble of similar cells from the same origin that together carry out a specific function. Organs are then formed by the functional grouping together of multiple tissues.

questions.question: Conjoint type of vascular bundles are common in:
questions.question_raw: Conjoint type of vascular bundles are common in:
questions.explanation: In the conjoint type of vascular bundles, the xylem and phloem are situated at the same radius of vascular bundles. Such vascular bundles are common in stems and leaves. The conjoint vascular bundles usually have the phloem located only on the outer side of the xylem.

questions.question: Which one is not a xylem cell?
questions.question_raw: Which one is not a xylem cell?
questions.explanation: Xylem is a complex tissue, which consists of four kinds of cells. These cells are tracheid, vessels, xylem parenchyma and xylem fibres. Companion cell is part of phloem tissue.

questions.question: Which is not the function of parenchymatous cells?
questions.question_raw: Which is not the function of parenchymatous cells?
questions.explanation: The parenchyma performs various functions like photosynthesis, storage, secretion.

questions.question: Which among the following are elongated or tube-like cells with thick and lignified walls and tapering ends?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following are elongated or tube-like cells with thick and lignified walls and tapering ends?
questions.explanation: Tracheids are elongated cells in the xylem of vascular plants that serve in the transport of water and mineral salts. Tracheids are one of two types of tracheary elements, vessel elements being the other.

questions.question: The characteristic feature of which cells have lignified thickening at the corner?
questions.question_raw: The characteristic feature of which cells have lignified thickening at the corner?
questions.explanation: Sclerenchyma consists of long, narrow cells with thick and lignified cell walls having a few or numerous pits.

questions.question: In monocotyledons, guard cell of stomata are The figure given below is the apical meristem in the root. Identify ‘A’ in the diagram.
questions.question_raw: In monocotyledons, guard cell of stomata are The figure given below is the apical meristem in the root. Identify ‘A’ in the diagram.
questions.explanation: The part ‘A’ is protoderm in apical meristem of root.

questions.question: Which is the function of companion cells?
questions.question_raw: Which is the function of companion cells?

options.label: B
options.text: To store food materials like resins, latex etc.

options.label: C
options.text: Controlling the functions of sieve tubes.

options.label: D
options.text: To maintain the pressure gradient in the sieve tubes.

correct_option_texts: Controlling the functions of sieve tubes.
questions.explanation: Controlling the functions of sieve tubes.

questions.question: Tracheids differ from vessels in having:
questions.question_raw: Tracheids differ from vessels in having:
