tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-01
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole & leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.question_raw: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole & leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.explanation: The blood plasma is filtered through the capillaries of the glomerulus into the capsule. The Bowman's capsule empties the filtrate into the proximal tubule that is also part of the duct system of the nephron. A glomerulus receives its blood supply from an afferent arteriole of the renal circulation.

questions.question: A renal corpuscle is:
questions.question_raw: A renal corpuscle is:
questions.explanation: Renal corpuscle, also called malpighian body, filtration unit of vertebrate nephrons, functional units of the kidney. It consists of a knot of capillaries (glomerulus) surrounded by a double-walled capsule (Bowman's capsule) that opens into a tubule.

questions.question: Homeostasis in the body is largely maintained by:
questions.question_raw: Homeostasis in the body is largely maintained by:
questions.explanation: Homeostasis is the maintenance of constant body temperature. It is mainly maintained by excretion and osmoregulation, which is the function of the kidney.

questions.question: The urinary bladder is absent in:
questions.question_raw: The urinary bladder is absent in:
questions.explanation: Urinary bladder is absent in snake and bird because the urine of these is semi-solid consisting mainly of uric acid. The primary function of the urinary bladder is to store urine in mammals. The urinary bladder cannot store semi-solid urine.

questions.question: Ascending limb of Henle’s loop is not permeable for:
questions.question_raw: Ascending limb of Henle’s loop is not permeable for:
questions.explanation: Ascending limb of Henle’s is not permeable to water. The descending limb is although permeable to water. Countercurrent exchange takes place between the loop of Henle’s and arteries surrounding it.

questions.question: The physiological unit of a kidney is:
questions.question_raw: The physiological unit of a kidney is:
questions.explanation: Nephrons are the physical unit or functional unit of the kidney. Millions of nephrons are present in each kidney but a physiological unit of the kidney is uriniferous tubules.

questions.question: The adult human kidney is metanephric. So, first embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are:
questions.question_raw: The adult human kidney is metanephric. So, first embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are:
questions.explanation: The adult human kidney is metanephric, the third and final excretory organ that develops in a vertebrate embryo. First embryonic tubules formed in vertebrates during embryonic development are pronephric.

questions.question: Urinary bladder helps to:
questions.question_raw: Urinary bladder helps to:

options.label: A
options.text: Stores urine for reabsorbing water from it

questions.explanation: The urinary bladder is a hollow elastic organ that functions as the body's urine storage tank. Urine produced by the kidneys flows through the ureters to the urinary bladder, where is it stored before passing into the urethra and exiting the body.

questions.question: The part of a nephron which adds some material to the filtrate is:
questions.question_raw: The part of a nephron which adds some material to the filtrate is:
questions.explanation: Each nephron consists of Bowman’s cup, tubules, and loops of Henle’s. The filtration of blood occurs in glomerulus cells present in Bowman’s cup. Some materials are added to the initial filtrate in the distal convoluted tubule.

questions.question:
In the human kidney:
 the outer zone is called the medulla and inner zone is called the cortex.
 cortex extends between medullary pyramids as renal columns of Bellini.
questions.question_raw:
In the human kidney:

  1. the outer zone is called the medulla and inner zone is called the cortex.
  2. cortex extends between medullary pyramids as renal columns of Bellini.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (ii) is wrong and (i) is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

questions.explanation: Inside the kidney, there are two zones, an outer cortex, and an inner medulla. The cortex extends in between the medullary pyramids as renal columns called columns of Bertini.

questions.question: Vasa recta is absent or reduced in:
questions.question_raw: Vasa recta is absent or reduced in:
questions.explanation: The efferent arteriole emerging from the glomerulus forms a fine capillary network around the renal tubule called the peritubular capillaries. A minute vessel of this network runs parallel to Henle’s loop forming a ‘U’ shaped vasa recta. Vasa recta are absent or highly reduced in cortical nephrons.

questions.question: Towards the centre of the inner concave surface of the kidney is a notch called:
questions.question_raw: Towards the centre of the inner concave surface of the kidney is a notch called:
questions.explanation: On the medial margin of the kidney is a concave region called the renal hilum. The renal hilum is the entrance to the renal sinus. Structures such as the renal veins, artery, nerves, and lymphatic vessels are located in the renal hilum.

questions.question: Label the following in diagram
questions.question_raw: Label the following in diagram
questions.explanation: The diagram shown above is Malpighian body or renal corpuscles. The marked part in this is called Podocytes or visceral epithelial cells in Bowman’s capsule in the kidney that warp around capillaries of the glomerules.

questions.question:
In the human excretory system:
 Kidneys and ureters are paired structures but the urinary bladder is single.
 Kidneys are situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra.
questions.question_raw:
In the human excretory system:

  1. Kidneys and ureters are paired structures but the urinary bladder is single.
  2. Kidneys are situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

questions.explanation: The excretory system in humans consists of a pair of kidneys, one pair of ureters, a urinary bladder, and a urethra. Kidneys are reddish-brown, bean-shaped structures situated between the levels of the last thoracic and third lumbar vertebra close to the dorsal inner wall of the abdominal cavity.

questions.question: In the following diagram what are 7,9 & 10 :
questions.question_raw: In the following diagram what are 7,9 & 10 :
questions.explanation: Towards the centre of the innerconcave surface of the kidney is a notchcalled hilum through which ureter, bloodvessels and nerves enter. Inner to the hilumis a broad funnel shaped space called therenal pelvis with projections called calyces. The medulla is divided into a few conical masses projecting into the calyces. Thus, 7, 9, and 10 are pelvis, hilum and calyx respectively.

tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-02
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole and leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.question_raw: Blood enters glomerular capillaries through ________ arteriole and leaves through ________ arteriole.
questions.explanation: Bowman cup contains glomerular capillaries to filter the blood. Blood enters the glomerular capillaries through afferent arteriole and leaves through the efferent arteriole.

questions.question: pH of urine under healthy conditions is:
questions.question_raw: pH of urine under healthy conditions is:
questions.explanation: The pH of urine under healthy conditions is slightly less than 7 so, slightly acidic in nature.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of glomerular filtrate?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of glomerular filtrate?
questions.explanation: Glomerular filtrate contains glucose, inorganic salts, and creatinine but it does not contain globulins protein.

questions.question: The epithelial cells of Bowman’s capsule are called:
questions.question_raw: The epithelial cells of Bowman’s capsule are called:
questions.explanation: Podocytes are cells in the Bowman's capsule in the kidneys that wrap around capillaries of the glomerulus. The Bowman's capsule filters the blood, retaining large molecules such as proteins while smaller molecules such as water, salts, and sugars are filtered as the first step in the formation of urine.

questions.question: Conditional reabsorption of sodium ions and water takes place in_______.
questions.question_raw: Conditional reabsorption of sodium ions and water takes place in_______.
questions.explanation: Distal Convoluted Tubule (DCT): Conditional reabsorption of Na^+ and water takes place in this segment. DCT is also capable of reabsorption of HCO_3^- and selective secretion of hydrogen and potassium ions and NH_3 to maintain the pH and sodium-potassium balance in the blood.

questions.question: The filtration fraction is the ratio of GFR to RPF where both the values are in ml/min and FF is expressed in percentage. Calculate FF for a normal adult human being, if RPF= 600ml/min:
questions.question_raw: The filtration fraction is the ratio of GFR to RPF where both the values are in ml/min and FF is expressed in percentage. Calculate FF for a normal adult human being, if RPF= 600ml/min:
questions.explanation:
Filtration fraction is the ratio of glomerular filtration rate (GFR) to the renal plasma flow (RPF). GFR of a normal human is 120 ml/min. so,
FF = ${GFR\over RPF} \times 100$
= ${120\over 600} \times 100$ = 20%.

questions.question: During urine formation, the tubular cells secrete ________ into the filtrate.
questions.question_raw: During urine formation, the tubular cells secrete ________ into the filtrate.

options.label: D
options.text: hydrogen ions, potassium ions and ammonia

correct_option_texts: hydrogen ions, potassium ions and ammonia
questions.explanation:
The substances that are secreted into the tubular fluid (for removal from the body) include:
 Potassium ions (K+),
 Hydrogen ions (H+),
 Ammonium ions (NH_4+),
 Creatinine,
 Urea,
 Some hormones,
 and some drugs (e.g. penicillin).

questions.question:
Counter current mechanism:
 It operates in all vertebrates.
 It produces hypertonic urine.
questions.question_raw:
Counter current mechanism:

  1. It operates in all vertebrates.
  2. It produces hypertonic urine.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct
questions.explanation: Countercurrent mechanism operates in mammals but not in all vertebrates. Due to the countercurrent mechanism, it produces hypertonic urine, highly concentrated.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about urine formation:
 Ultrafiltration doesn’t need any energy expenditure by kidney cells.
 Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, urea, hormones, amino acids, etc takes place through passive transport.
 PCT cells have many microvilli along with few mitochondria since most of the nutrients are absorbed passively.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about urine formation:

  1. Ultrafiltration doesn’t need any energy expenditure by kidney cells.
  2. Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, urea, hormones, amino acids, etc takes place through passive transport.
  3. PCT cells have many microvilli along with few mitochondria since most of the nutrients are absorbed passively.
questions.explanation: During urine formation, ultrafiltration takes place in which energy is not required by cells of the kidney. Selective reabsorption of glucose, water, salt, etc. occurs actively and proximal convoluted tubules (PCT) do not have microvilli.

questions.question: If in glomerular capillaries, pressure of blood is 75 mm Hg, osmotic pressure of plasma proteins is 30 mm Hg, and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule is 25 mm Hg then net force or the filtration pressure will be calculated as :
questions.question_raw: If in glomerular capillaries, pressure of blood is 75 mm Hg, osmotic pressure of plasma proteins is 30 mm Hg, and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule is 25 mm Hg then net force or the filtration pressure will be calculated as :
questions.explanation: Net force of filtration pressure is equal to the blood pressure of glomerular capillaries minus sum of the osmotic pressure of plasma and hydrostatic pressure of fluid in renal tubule. So, filtration pressure = 75 – [ 30 + 20 ] mm Hg.

questions.question: The hormone with enzymatic action which catalyses the conversion of angiotensinogen into angiotensin is:
questions.question_raw: The hormone with enzymatic action which catalyses the conversion of angiotensinogen into angiotensin is:
questions.explanation: A fall in glomerular blood flow/glomerular blood pressure/GFR can activate the JG cells to release renin which converts angiotensinogen in the blood to angiotensin I and further to angiotensin II.

questions.question: Vasopressin is a hormone involved in the regulation of urine formation. It is secreted from:
questions.question_raw: Vasopressin is a hormone involved in the regulation of urine formation. It is secreted from:
questions.explanation: An excessive loss of fluid from the body can activate receptors that stimulate the hypothalamus to release antidiuretic hormone (ADH) or vasopressin from the neurohypophysis.

questions.question: Select the mismatched pair of the following:
questions.question_raw: Select the mismatched pair of the following:

options.label: A
options.text: Cortical Nephron - About 25% of total nephrons

options.label: B
options.text: Glomerular Filtration Rate - 180 litres per day

options.label: D
options.text: Juxtamedullary Nephron - About 15% of total nephrons

questions.explanation: A cortical nephron is about 25% of total nephrons. The glomerular filtration rate is 180 litre per day. Juxtamedullary nephron makes about 15% of total nephrons but the lung does not remove 180 L of carbon dioxide per day.

questions.question: What are the unlabeled a, b & c in the given flow chart?
questions.question_raw: What are the unlabeled a, b & c in the given flow chart?
questions.explanation: Renin convert angiotensin I to angiotensin II which activates adrenal cortex to release aldosterone. Aldosterone helps to reabsorption of sodium and water from DCT.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about renal threshold:
 The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100ml of blood.
 If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.
 If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes mellitus.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about renal threshold:

  1. The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100ml of blood.
  2. If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.
  3. If the blood level of glucose exceeds 180mg, it leads to diabetes mellitus.
questions.explanation: The renal threshold is the state when glucose is present in urine. The renal threshold of glucose is 180 mg/100 ml of blood. If the blood glucose level exceeds 180 mg, it leads to diabetes insipidus.

tests.testPaperName: Excretory Products And Their Elimination Test-03
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Excretory Products and their Elimination

questions.question: During micturition, the muscles of urinary bladder, and urethral sphincters will:
questions.question_raw: During micturition, the muscles of urinary bladder, and urethral sphincters will:
questions.explanation: The CNS passes on motor messages to initiate the contraction of smooth muscles of the bladder and simultaneous relaxation of the urethral sphincter causing the release of urine.

questions.question: In the following pictorial representation of Kreb’s Hensleit cycle, find out 1,2& 3?
questions.question_raw: In the following pictorial representation of Kreb’s Hensleit cycle, find out 1,2& 3?
questions.explanation: Kreb’s Hensleit cycle is the sequence of chemical reaction occurring primarily in the liver that results in the production of urea. In this cycle ammonia is fist converted into citrulline along with carbon dioxide that change into arginine and finally into ornithine.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 Infants and toddlers are often under the habit of bed wetting since micturition is a reflex process.
 Adults and grown-up children can control this reflex process, voluntarily to some extent.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. Infants and toddlers are often under the habit of bed wetting since micturition is a reflex process.
  2. Adults and grown-up children can control this reflex process, voluntarily to some extent.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

questions.explanation: The process of release of urine is called micturition and the neural mechanism causing it is called the micturition reflex. Adults and grown-up children can control the reflex process of micturition voluntarily to some extent.

questions.question: All of the following organs help in excretion except:
questions.question_raw: All of the following organs help in excretion except:
questions.explanation: The lungs help in the excretion of carbon dioxide, the liver help in the excretion of the toxic substance, and the skin help in the removal of extra salts along with sweat. The heart is the organ that pumps the blood in the body.

questions.question: Urea cycle operates in:
questions.question_raw: Urea cycle operates in:
questions.explanation: Urea cycles operate in the liver, where ammonia is converted into urea. Sometimes urea cycle also occurs in the kidney.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle.
 Birds are unable to make urine as hypotonic as mammals can.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle.
  2. Birds are unable to make urine as hypotonic as mammals can.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: Avian kidneys have very short loops of Henle’s. Loops of Henle’s used in the concentration of urine. Birds make urine highly hypotonic.

questions.question: If kidney tissues are supplied with blood low in oxygen then:
questions.question_raw: If kidney tissues are supplied with blood low in oxygen then:

options.label: A
options.text: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops

options.label: C
options.text: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration continue normally

correct_option_texts: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops
questions.explanation: Both tubular reabsorption and ultrafiltration stops

questions.question: Diabetes insipidus is caused due to:
questions.question_raw: Diabetes insipidus is caused due to:
questions.explanation: Diabetes insipidus is a rate variety of diabetes characterized by excessive thrust and excretion of a large amount of urine. It is caused due to reduced secretion of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) or vasopressin hormone.

questions.question: Rahul is anaemic and a few days ago suffered from massive haemorrhage. Now, what problems he may face:
questions.question_raw: Rahul is anaemic and a few days ago suffered from massive haemorrhage. Now, what problems he may face:

options.label: B
options.text: Accumulation of waste products in body

questions.explanation: If the anaemic person suffered from massive haemorrhage, the problem he may face includes very low blood pressure, accumulation of wastes products in the body, and no glomerular filtration in the kidney.

questions.question: Reema is on dialysis and she is unable to conceive due to high levels of waste products in her body fluids. Her doctor can suggest her ________.
questions.question_raw: Reema is on dialysis and she is unable to conceive due to high levels of waste products in her body fluids. Her doctor can suggest her ________.
questions.explanation: When both kidneys of a person get damaged and not able to filter the blood, dialysis is used to remove nitrogenous wastes. High wastes accumulation is unable a woman to conceive. The ultimate solution to this problem is a kidney transplant.

questions.question: People with chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. It happens because a damaged kidney doesn’t secrete sufficient:
questions.question_raw: People with chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. It happens because a damaged kidney doesn’t secrete sufficient:
questions.explanation: The person suffering from chronic kidney disease are usually affected by anaemia. The damaged kidney is not able to secrete sufficient erythropoietin in the blood that helps in the absorption of iron.

questions.question:
Match the kidney disorders and its symptoms:
 (a) Ketosis
 (i) frequent thirst
 (b) Polydipsia
 (ii) blood in urine
 (c) Alkaptonuria
 (iii) acetoacetic acid in urine
 (d) Haematuria
 (iv) homogentisic acid in urine
questions.question_raw:
Match the kidney disorders and its symptoms:

(a) Ketosis (i) frequent thirst
(b) Polydipsia (ii) blood in urine
(c) Alkaptonuria (iii) acetoacetic acid in urine
(d) Haematuria (iv) homogentisic acid in urine
questions.explanation: Ketosis is the presence of acetoacetic acid in the urine, polydipsia is the condition of frequent thirst, alkaptonuria is the presence of homogentisic acid in urine and haematuria is the presence of blood in the urine.

questions.question: Dev consumes a large amount of alcohol and the result is polyuria and dehydration due to:
questions.question_raw: Dev consumes a large amount of alcohol and the result is polyuria and dehydration due to:

options.label: A
options.text: Increase in the level of vasopressin

options.label: B
options.text: Decrease in the level of vasopressin

options.label: C
options.text: Decrease in the level of ANF

options.label: D
options.text: Decrease in the level of aldosterone

correct_option_texts: Decrease in the level of vasopressin
questions.explanation: Polyuria is usually the result of drinking excessive amounts of fluids (polydipsia), particularly water and fluids that contain caffeine or alcohol. A person suffering from polyuria may develop excessive production of urea and suffer from dehydration due to a decrease in the level of antidiuretic hormone or vasopressin.

questions.question: Which one of the following is correct with reference to haemodialysis?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is correct with reference to haemodialysis?

options.label: A
options.text: Anti-heparin is added prior to haemodialysis.

options.label: B
options.text: It absorbs and resends excess of ions.

options.label: C
options.text: The dialysis unit has a coiled semi permeable cellophane tube.

options.label: D
options.text: Nitrogenous wastes are removed by active transport

correct_option_texts: The dialysis unit has a coiled semi permeable cellophane tube.
questions.explanation: In haemodialysis, blood drained from a convenient artery is pumped into a dialysing unit after adding an anticoagulant like heparin. The unit contains a coiled cellophane tube surrounded by a fluid (dialysing fluid) having the same composition as that of plasma except for the nitrogenous wastes. The porous cellophane membrane of the tube allows the passage of molecules based on concentration gradient. As nitrogenous wastes are absent in the dialysing fluid, these substances freely move out, thereby clearing the blood. The cleared blood is pumped back to the body through a vein after adding anti-heparin to it.

 Excretory organ

Animal Excretory organ

chapters.name: Locomotion and Movement

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Locomotion and Movement

questions.question: Actin filaments are found in all eukaryotic cells but reportedly absent in:
questions.question_raw: Actin filaments are found in all eukaryotic cells but reportedly absent in:
questions.explanation: Actin filament help in the contraction and relaxation of muscles. Actin filament is found in all eukaryotic cells but it is absent in nematode sperms.

questions.question: Study of muscles is called:
questions.question_raw: Study of muscles is called:
questions.explanation: Myology is the study of the muscular system, including the study of the structure, function, and diseases of muscle.

questions.question: The correct organization of skeletal muscle is:
questions.question_raw: The correct organization of skeletal muscle is:

options.label: B
options.text: muscle bundles $ \to $ myofibrils $ \to $ muscle cells $ \to $ sarcomere

options.label: D
options.text: muscle bundles $ \to $ muscle cells $ \to $ muscle fibres $ \to $ sarcomere

questions.explanation: Each organized skeletal muscle in our body is made of a number of muscle bundles or fascicles held together by a common collagenous connective tissue layer called fascia. Each muscle bundle contains a number of muscle fibers. A characteristic feature of the muscle fiber is the presence of a large number of parallelly arranged filaments in the sarcoplasm called myofilaments or myofibrils. The portion of the myofibril between two successive ‘Z’ lines is considered as the functional unit of contraction and is called a sarcomere.

questions.question: Discovery of actin is credited to_______.
questions.question_raw: Discovery of actin is credited to_______.
questions.explanation: Actin and myosin filaments are responsible for the contraction and relaxation of the muscle. The discovery of actin is credited to Straub.

questions.question: Muscles are attached to bones via:
questions.question_raw: Muscles are attached to bones via:
questions.explanation: Muscles are attached to bones by tendons and bones are attached to another bone by ligaments. Tendon and ligaments are types of connective tissue.

questions.question: A functional G-actin molecule in its free state is bound to:
questions.question_raw: A functional G-actin molecule in its free state is bound to:
questions.explanation: A functional G-actin molecule in its free state is bound to magnesium ion and ATP. The G-actin molecule forms a bridge between the muscle fibres for sliding.

questions.question: A meromyosin molecule doesn’t contain:
questions.question_raw: A meromyosin molecule doesn’t contain:
questions.explanation: Each meromyosin has two important parts, a globular head with a short arm and a tail.

questions.question: The property which doesn’t belong to muscle fibres is:
questions.question_raw: The property which doesn’t belong to muscle fibres is:
questions.explanation: Muscle fibres have properties of excitability, elasticity, and contractility but conductivity is not present in muscle fibers. These fibres help in the movement of different body parts.

questions.question:
Myoglobin is found to be:
 highly abundant in muscles of whales and seals.
 exhibiting cooperative binding of oxygen.
 allowing organisms to hold their breath longer underwater.
questions.question_raw:
Myoglobin is found to be:

  1. highly abundant in muscles of whales and seals.
  2. exhibiting cooperative binding of oxygen.
  3. allowing organisms to hold their breath longer underwater.
questions.explanation: Myoglobin is found to be highly abundant in muscles of whales and seals. Myoglobin being oxygen and iron-binding protein allowing organisms to hold their breath longer underwater.

questions.question: Polymerisation of actin monomers involves ATP hydrolysis as shown in the following figure: [choose the correct one]
questions.question_raw: Polymerisation of actin monomers involves ATP hydrolysis as shown in the following figure: [choose the correct one]
questions.explanation: Each ‘F’ actin is a polymer of monomeric ‘G’ (Globular) actins.

questions.question: The correct result of ATPase activity that occurs during muscle contraction is :
questions.question_raw: The correct result of ATPase activity that occurs during muscle contraction is :
questions.explanation: ATPase enzyme acts on myosin to act on ATP to convert into ADP. The activated myosin combine with actin for contraction.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about muscle contraction in humans:
 chemical energy is converted into mechanical energy during muscle contraction and the chemical is a neurotransmitter.
 neurotransmitter acetylcholine at the motor end plate converts electrical energy into mechanical energy.
 in a contracted muscle, volume remains the same.
 a contracted muscle becomes shorter and thicker.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about muscle contraction in humans:

  1. chemical energy is converted into mechanical energy during muscle contraction and the chemical is a neurotransmitter.
  2. neurotransmitter acetylcholine at the motor end plate converts electrical energy into mechanical energy.
  3. in a contracted muscle, volume remains the same.
  4. a contracted muscle becomes shorter and thicker.
questions.explanation: The chemical energy is converted into mechanical energy during muscle contraction and the chemical is a neurotransmitter. In a contracted muscle, volume remains the same and a contracted muscle becomes shorter and thicker.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are incorrect about sliding filament theory of muscle contraction?

options.label: B
options.text: During the contraction width of the H zone decreases.

options.label: C
options.text: During contraction width of I and A bands remain the same.

options.label: D
options.text: During contraction width of I and A bands remain the same and During contraction width of H zone decreases.

correct_option_texts: During contraction width of I and A bands remain the same.
questions.explanation: During contraction, the ‘I’ bands get reduced, whereas the ‘A’ bands retain the length.

questions.question: Myoglobin was the first protein to have its three-dimensional structure revealed. Ravi was diagnosed with myoglobin in his bloodstream. This had occurred due to:
questions.question_raw: Myoglobin was the first protein to have its three-dimensional structure revealed. Ravi was diagnosed with myoglobin in his bloodstream. This had occurred due to:
questions.explanation: Myoglobin is an iron and oxygen-binding protein found in the muscle tissue of mammals. The presence of myoglobin in the bloodstream may be due to muscle injury of Ravi due to accident or sudden jerk.

questions.question: The vertebral formula of human is:
questions.question_raw: The vertebral formula of human is:
questions.explanation: The vertebral column is differentiated into cervical (7), thoracic (12), lumbar (5), sacral (5-fused) and coccygeal (4-fused) regions starting from the skull.

questions.question: At the base of skull, a large hole is present which allows brain to continue with the spinal cord. That hole is called :
questions.question_raw: At the base of skull, a large hole is present which allows brain to continue with the spinal cord. That hole is called :
questions.explanation: Foramen Magnum is the largest foramen situated on the base of the skull through which multiple structures pass including the Spinal Cord. It acts as a passage between the cranial vault and the base of the skull.

questions.question: Axial skeleton consists of:
questions.question_raw: Axial skeleton consists of:
questions.explanation: The axial skeleton is the part of the skeleton that consists of the bones of the head and trunk of a vertebrate. In the human skeleton, it consists of 80 bones and is composed of six parts; the skull bones, the ossicles of the middle ear, the hyoid bone, the rib cage, the sternum, and the vertebral column.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Vertebra
 No.of bones feature
 (a) Cervical
 (i) 5
 (b) Coccyx
 (ii) 4
 (c) Thoracic
 (iii) 12
 (d) Lumbar
 (iv) 7
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Vertebra No.of bones feature
(a) Cervical (i) 5
(b) Coccyx (ii) 4
(c) Thoracic (iii) 12
(d) Lumbar (iv) 7
questions.explanation: The vertebral column usually consists of 33 vertebrae: 24 presacral vertebrae (7 cervical, 12 thoracic, and 5 lumbar) followed by the sacrum (5 fused sacral vertebrae) and the coccyx (4 frequently fused coccygeal vertebrae).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of hip bone?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of hip bone?
questions.explanation: Hip bone or pelvic girdle consists of two coxal bones. Each coxal bone is formed by the fusion of three bones- ilium, ischium, and pubis.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of coxal bone?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of coxal bone?
questions.explanation: The coxal bone (hip bone, pelvic bone) is a large, flattened, irregularly shaped bone, constricted in the center and expanded above and below. It consists of three parts, the ilium, ischium, and pubis, which are distinct from each other in the young subject but are fused in the adult.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Animal
 Type of skeleton
 (a) Sphyrna
 (i) exoskeleton
 (b) Pediculus
 (ii) hydrostatic skeleton
 (c) Asterias
 (iii) cartilaginous skeleton
 (d) Metridium
 (iv) endoskeleton
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Animal Type of skeleton
(a) Sphyrna (i) exoskeleton
(b) Pediculus (ii) hydrostatic skeleton
(c) Asterias (iii) cartilaginous skeleton
(d) Metridium (iv) endoskeleton
questions.explanation: Different kinds of skeleton systems are found in different organisms. Sphyma contains a cartilaginous skeleton. Pediculus contains an exoskeleton. Asterias contain endoskeleton and Metridium contains a hydrostatic skeleton.

questions.question: The upright posture in humans is facilitated by:
questions.question_raw: The upright posture in humans is facilitated by:

options.label: B
options.text: Massive hindlimb bones making broad feet

options.label: D
options.text: Massive hind limb bones making broad feet and pelvis

correct_option_texts: Massive hind limb bones making broad feet and pelvis
questions.explanation: The upright posture in humans is facilitated by massive hind limbs bones that make broad feet and pelvis to withhold the weight of whole body.

questions.question:
Each pectoral girdle:
 it has 2 pairs of bones, a pair of the clavicle and a pair of scapula.
 scapula contains the expanded process and glenoid cavity, the latter is for the articulation of the head of humerus.

Each pectoral girdle:

  1. it has 2 pairs of bones, a pair of the clavicle and a pair of scapula.
  2. scapula contains the expanded process and glenoid cavity, the latter is for the articulation of the head of humerus.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: C

questions.question:

 (a) Hipbone
 (i) patella
 (b) Kneebone
 (ii) clavicle

 (iv) innominate
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

(a) Hipbone (i) patella
(b) Kneebone (ii) clavicle
(c) Collarbone (iii) suture
(d) Immovable joint (iv) innominate
questions.explanation: Hip bone is called as innominate bone, the knee bone is called as patella, collarbone is called as clavicle and immovable joint is called a suture.

questions.question: Select the correctly matched pair:
questions.question_raw: Select the correctly matched pair:

correct_option_texts: 11th and 12th pair of ribs → floating ribs
questions.explanation: The last 2 pairs i.e., 11th and 12th of ribs are not connected ventrally and are, therefore, called floating ribs.

questions.question: In a woman, the pelvis is larger and has a broader front than a man. It is:
questions.question_raw: In a woman, the pelvis is larger and has a broader front than a man. It is:

options.label: B
options.text: Responsible for problems during the menstrual cycle

options.label: C
options.text: An adaptation for gestation and parturition

correct_option_texts: An adaptation for gestation and parturition
questions.explanation: In a woman, the pelvis is larger and has a broader front than a man. It is an adaptation for gestation and parturition during pregnancy.

 Cartilaginous joint is often called symphysis and eg. joints between adjacent vertebrae.

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: The joint between the adjacent vertebrae in the vertebral column is caritilaginous joint.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 The synovial joints are characterized by the presence of a fluid-filled synovial cavity between the articulating surfaces of the two bones.
 The saddle is a type of synovial joint.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. The synovial joints are characterized by the presence of a fluid-filled synovial cavity between the articulating surfaces of the two bones.
  2. The saddle is a type of synovial joint.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

questions.explanation: Synovial joints are characterized by the presence of a fluid-filled synovial cavity between the articulating surfaces of the two bones. A saddle joint is a synovial joint where one of the bones forming the joint is shaped like a saddle with the other bone resting on it like a rider on a horse. Saddle joint (between carpal and metacarpal of thumb).

questions.explanation: The X-ray of knee shows gout in the knee. Gout is a kind of arthritis. It can cause an attack of sudden burning pain, stiffness and swelling in joint.

questions.question: The joint present between the carpals is
questions.question_raw: The joint present between the carpals is
questions.explanation: Many gliding joints are formed in the appendicular skeleton between the carpal bones of the wrist; between the carpals and the metacarpals of the palm; between the tarsal bones of the ankle; and between the tarsals and the metatarsals of the foot.

(c) Ball and socket joint (iii)

questions.explanation: Fibrous joint is present in skull bone, hinge joint is present in knee, ball and socket joint is present in shoulder and forelimb and ellipsoid joint is present in wrist of human beings.

questions.question:
Read the following statements and view carefully the figure:
Osteoporosis is an age-related disorder characterised by decreased bone mass. Osteoarthritis is shown in the following figure.
The above figure shows gouty arthritis which is caused by inflammation of joints.
Decreased levels of estrogen is a common cause for increased chances of fractures.
Inflammation of joints is caused by decreased levels of calcium ions in body fluids.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements and view carefully the figure:

  1. Osteoporosis is an age-related disorder characterised by decreased bone mass. Osteoarthritis is shown in the following figure.
  2. The above figure shows gouty arthritis which is caused by inflammation of joints.
  3. Decreased levels of estrogen is a common cause for increased chances of fractures.
  4. Inflammation of joints is caused by decreased levels of calcium ions in body fluids.
questions.explanation: Osteoporosis is an age-related disorder characterised by decreased bone mass. It is mainly caused by decreased level of estrogen in blood. Osteoporosis may leads to increased chances of fracture.

questions.question:
In the following two graphs:
 the first graph shows: twitch $ \to $ summation $ \to $ etanus $ \to $ fatigue.
 the second graph shows incomplete and complete tetanus.
questions.question_raw:
In the following two graphs:

  1. the first graph shows: twitch  {tex} \to {/tex} summation {tex} \to {/tex}  etanus {tex} \to {/tex} fatigue.
  2. the second graph shows incomplete and complete tetanus.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (ii) is wrong and (i) is correct.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is wrong.

questions.explanation:
Graph 1 shows muscle fibre tension with time. Which occurs in order as: twitch $ \to $ summation $ \to $ tetanus $ \to $ fatigue.
Graph 2 shows complete and incomplete tetanus. Tetanus is also known as lockjaw, which is a infection characterized by muscle spasm.

questions.question: Aging will be characterised by:
questions.question_raw: Aging will be characterised by:

options.label: D
options.text: Decreased muscle size and increased muscle fat

correct_option_texts: Decreased muscle size and increased muscle fat
questions.explanation: Aging will be characterized by decreased muscle size and increased muscle fat. With aging muscle loose its contractile and elasticity nature. Fats are deposited in muscle fibres the further decrease contracting ability.

questions.question:
Muscular dystrophy is a genetic disorder:
 It is genetically inherited.
 Some people get affected by MD due to the mutated dystrophin gene and nutritional defects at the pre-natal age.
 Predominantly males are affected by DMD although females can be carriers of it.
 Dystrophin is a protein found in tendons.
questions.question_raw:
Muscular dystrophy is a genetic disorder:

  1. It is genetically inherited.
  2. Some people get affected by MD due to the mutated dystrophin gene and nutritional defects at the pre-natal age.
  3. Predominantly males are affected by DMD although females can be carriers of it.
  4. Dystrophin is a protein found in tendons.
questions.explanation: Muscular dystrophy is a genetic disorder and genetically inherited. It may also occur due to mutated dystrophin gene and nutritional defects at the pre-natal age. Males are more affected by MD although females can be carriers of it.

questions.question:
Read the following about joint replacement therapy :
 Artificial joints are made from a similar material that makes our bones.
 Metal or plastic is used to prepare an artificial joint.
 The following figure shows a dysfunctional joint in the knee.
 Above figure shows an artificial joint in the knee.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following about joint replacement therapy :

  1. Artificial joints are made from a similar material that makes our bones.
  2. Metal or plastic is used to prepare an artificial joint.
  3. The following figure shows a dysfunctional joint in the knee.
  4. Above figure shows an artificial joint in the knee.
questions.explanation: Artificial joints are made up of metal and plastic. The figure shown above shows artificial joint in the knee.

questions.question: Sachin suffers from hypoparathyroidism. This can lead him to:
questions.question_raw: Sachin suffers from hypoparathyroidism. This can lead him to:
questions.explanation: Hypoparathyroidism is the condition in which the body secretes abnormally low level of parathyroid hormone. This deficiency may lead to hypocalcemia and tetany.

questions.question: Which disorder is caused due to wild contractions in muscles?
questions.question_raw: Which disorder is caused due to wild contractions in muscles?
questions.explanation: Tetany is caused due to rapid spasms (wild contractions) in muscle due to low Ca^++ in body fluid.

questions.question:
During vigorous exercise:
 Lactic acid accumulates in muscle cells to meet its energy needs immediately.
 Lactic acid is formed by anaerobic respiration hence oxygen consumption by muscles is reduced.
 This reduction in oxygen consumption is called oxygen debt of muscle.
questions.question_raw:
During vigorous exercise:

  1. Lactic acid accumulates in muscle cells to meet its energy needs immediately.
  2. Lactic acid is formed by anaerobic respiration hence oxygen consumption by muscles is reduced.
  3. This reduction in oxygen consumption is called oxygen debt of muscle.
questions.explanation: During vigorous exercise, lactic acid accumulates in muscles cells to meet the energy requirement of muscle cell. Lactic acid is formed due to insufficient availability of oxygen.

questions.question: Myasthenia gravis leads to fatigue and weakness. It is not:
questions.question_raw: Myasthenia gravis leads to fatigue and weakness. It is not:
questions.explanation: Myasthenia gravis is an autoimmune disorder affecting neuromuscular junction leading to fatigue, weakening, and paralysis of skeletal muscle.

questions.question:
A 5-year-old girl Sita and her grandmother together met an accident. Her grandmother got her leg fractured but Sita didn’t suffer from any fractures:
 At a young age, bones are more flexible and contain lots of organic matter.
 Sita has a more powerful immune system than her grandmother.
 In old people bones become hard and brittle.
questions.question_raw:
A 5-year-old girl Sita and her grandmother together met an accident. Her grandmother got her leg fractured but Sita didn’t suffer from any fractures:

  1. At a young age, bones are more flexible and contain lots of organic matter.
  2. Sita has a more powerful immune system than her grandmother.
  3. In old people bones become hard and brittle.
questions.explanation: Generally, bones are flexible at a young age and become hard and brittle at old age.

chapters.name: Neural Control and Coordination
chapters.full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Neural Control and Coordination

questions.question: Nissl’s granules are absent in:
questions.question_raw: Nissl’s granules are absent in:
questions.explanation: Nissl’s grains are mainly present in cyton and dendrite. These granules are rough endoplasmic reticulum with rosettes of the free ribosome, the site for protein synthesis. It is absent in the axon.

questions.question: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.question_raw: In the prosencephalon, first, and second ventricles open into the third ventricle by:
questions.explanation: In prosencephalon, the first and second ventricles open into third ventricles by foramen of Monro. It is the opening that connects the left and right sides of the lateral ventricle in the brain.

questions.question: The property which doesn’t belong to nerve fibres is:
questions.question_raw: The property which doesn’t belong to nerve fibres is:
questions.explanation: Nerve fibres are specialized to transmit impulses from one part of the body to others. Nerve fibres do not show the property of elasticity and contractility.

questions.question: Hypothalamus helps to maintain homeostasis by controlling hunger, thirst, blood pressure, temperature feelings, etc. So it is an important link between:
questions.question_raw: Hypothalamus helps to maintain homeostasis by controlling hunger, thirst, blood pressure, temperature feelings, etc. So it is an important link between:
questions.explanation: One of the most important functions of the hypothalamus is to link the nervous system to the endocrine system via the pituitary gland (hypophysis). The hypothalamus is located below the thalamus and is part of the limbic system.

questions.question: Brain stem is the support system of brain and is the collective name for:
questions.question_raw: Brain stem is the support system of brain and is the collective name for:
questions.explanation: The brainstem (or brain stem) is the posterior part of the brain, adjoining and structurally continuous with the spinal cord. In the human brain, the brainstem includes the midbrain, the pons, and the medulla oblongata. Sometimes the diencephalon, the caudal part of the forebrain, is included.

questions.question:
Match the following :
 Column A
 Column B
 a) Unipolar nerves
 i) Nerve cells of retina
 b) Mixed nerves
 ii) Nerve cells of cerebral cortex
 c) bipolar nerves
 iii) Nerves of embryonic stage
 d) Multipolar nerves
 iv) All the spinal nerves
questions.question_raw:
Match the following :
 

Column A Column B
a) Unipolar nerves i) Nerve cells of retina
b) Mixed nerves ii) Nerve cells of cerebral cortex
c) bipolar nerves iii) Nerves of embryonic stage
d) Multipolar nerves iv) All the spinal nerves
questions.explanation: Unipolar nerves are present in nerves of embryonic stage. Mixed nerves are found in all the spinal nerves. Bipolar nerves are present in nerve cells of retina. Multipolarnerves are present in nerve cells of cerebral cortex.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Column A
 Column B
 (a) Motor Neuron
 (i) carry impulse away from cyton
 (b) Axon
 (ii) carry impulse from receptor to CNS
 (c) Afferent Neuron
 (iii) carry impulse towards cyton
 (d) Dendrite
 (iv) carry impulse from CNS to effector
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Column A Column B
(a) Motor Neuron (i) carry impulse away from cyton
(b) Axon (ii) carry impulse from receptor to CNS
(c) Afferent Neuron (iii) carry impulse towards cyton
(d) Dendrite (iv) carry impulse from CNS to effector
questions.explanation: Motor neurons carry impulse from CNS to the effector. Axon carries the impulse away from cyton. Afferent neuron carries impulse from receptor to CNS and dendrite carry impulse towards cyton.

questions.question: Complete the following hierarchical chart showing parts of the nervous system :
questions.question_raw: Complete the following hierarchical chart showing parts of the nervous system :
questions.explanation: The human nervous system consists of the central nervous system and peripheral nervous system. The peripheral nervous system includes 12 pairs of cranial nerves emerging from the brain and 31 pairs of spinal nerves arising from a spinal nerve.

questions.question:

i & ii (from up to down)
is a presynaptic membrane and
is a postsynaptic membrane.
synaptic vesicles are in postsynaptic knob.
neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane
questions.question_raw:
In the following figure a synapse is shown :

i & ii (from up to down)

  1.  
    1. is a presynaptic membrane and
    2. is a postsynaptic membrane.
  2. synaptic vesicles are in postsynaptic knob.
  3. neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane
questions.explanation: The synapse is formed by the membranes of apre-synaptic neuron and a post-synaptic neuron, which may or may not be separated by a gap called synaptic cleft. The neurotransmitter receptors are located on the postsynaptic membrane.

questions.question: Due to refractory period, a nerve fibre in human body on an average transmits about:
questions.question_raw: Due to refractory period, a nerve fibre in human body on an average transmits about:
questions.explanation: The refractory period is the time taken to nerve become ready to conduct other impulses. Due to this refractory period, a nerve fibre in the human body on average transmits about 1000 impulses per second.

questions.question:
Read the following about the surface of cerebral hemispheres:
 Is made up of the pia mater.
 Is greatly convoluted and called the cerebral cortex.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following about the surface of cerebral hemispheres:

  1. Is made up of the pia mater.
  2. Is greatly convoluted and called the cerebral cortex.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct
questions.explanation: The layer of cells which covers the cerebral hemisphere is called the cerebral cortex and is thrown into prominent folds.

questions.question:
The autonomic nervous system or ANS:
 Is truly autonomic i.e. independent of the CNS.
 Is also known as the VNS.
 Transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.
questions.question_raw:
The autonomic nervous system or ANS:

  1. Is truly autonomic i.e. independent of the CNS.
  2. Is also known as the VNS.
  3. Transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.
questions.explanation: The autonomic nervous system or ANS is the division of the peripheral nervous system that influences the function of internal organs. ANS is also known as VNS. These nerves transmit impulse from CNS to involuntary organs and smooth muscles.

questions.question: Label the arrows in the given diagram of the brain from top to bottom :
questions.question_raw: Label the arrows in the given diagram of the brain from top to bottom :
questions.explanation: The part of the human brain shown above has marked as cerebellum, pons and medulla oblongata of the hindbrain.

questions.question: The band of nerve fibers that joins the two cerebral hemisphere in mammals is:
questions.question_raw: The band of nerve fibers that joins the two cerebral hemisphere in mammals is:
questions.explanation: The corpus callosum (CC) is the largest of the commissural fibres, linking the cerebral cortex of the left and right cerebral hemisphere.

questions.question: Vermis is a portion of:
questions.question_raw: Vermis is a portion of:
questions.explanation: The cerebral vermis is located in the medial, cortico-nuclear zone of the cerebellum. It is constricted between the lobe of the cerebellum.
